levels.grammarNote: ser = identity, origin, profession, material, and telling time. yo soy, tú eres, él es, nosotros somos, ellos son. Tip: if you can ask 'what kind of thing is it?' → ser.
estar = temporary states, emotions, location, and progressive tenses. yo estoy, tú estás, él está. Location always uses estar — even for permanent buildings.

levels.front: I'm tired but I'm in a good mood.
levels.back: Estoy cansado pero estoy de buen humor.
levels.literal: [I] am tired but [I] am in good mood.

levels.grammarNote: tener is irregular: yo tengo, tú tienes, él tiene. Also used for age: 'Tengo 30 años' (I'm 30 years old) and physical states: tengo hambre/sed/frío/calor.
hacer is irregular in yo: hago. Used in weather: 'Hace calor/frío' (It's hot/cold). 'hacer una pregunta' = to ask a question.

levels.front: What do you do on weekends?
levels.literal: What do [you] the ends of week?

levels.grammarNote: ir is fully irregular: voy, vas, va, vamos, van. 'ir a + infinitive' = going to do something: 'voy a comer' (I'm going to eat).
poder stem-changes o→ue: puedo, puedes, puede, podemos, pueden.
querer stem-changes e→ie: quiero, quieres, quiere. Also means 'to love' with people: 'Te quiero' (I love you — less intense than te amo).
decir is highly irregular: digo, dices, dice. Preterite: dije, dijiste. 'quiero decir' = I mean. 'dime' = tell me.
saber = knowing facts or how to do something. Irregular yo: sé. '¿Sabes cocinar?' (Do you know how to cook?). Compare: conocer = to know people/places.

levels.front: Do you know how to cook?

levels.grammarNote: ver is irregular in yo: veo. Also means 'to watch': 'ver una película' (to watch a movie). 'a ver' = let's see (very common filler).
dar is irregular: yo doy, tú das. 'dame' = give me (extremely common). 'dar un paseo' = to take a walk, 'dar las gracias' = to thank.
'llegar a' = to arrive at/to. 'llegar tarde/temprano' = to arrive late/early. Note: llegar is also used where English says 'to get to': '¿Llegas a entenderlo?' (Do you manage to understand it?).
'creo que' = I think that (very common). 'No creo' = I don't think so. creer en = to believe in: 'creo en ti' (I believe in you).

levels.front: I think it's going to rain.
To happen / to pass / to spend (time)
levels.grammarNote: '¿Qué pasó?' (What happened?) is one of the most common questions. Also: 'pasar tiempo' (to spend time), 'pasar por' (to pass by/through), 'pase' (come in).
día is masculine despite ending in -a: el día, un día, todos los días (every day). Compare: la noche (night), la tarde (afternoon).
'llevar + time + gerund' = to have been doing something: 'Llevo dos años estudiando español' (I've been studying Spanish for two years). Also: el año pasado (last year), el año que viene (next year).

levels.front: I've been studying Spanish for two years.

levels.grammarNote: tiempo means both 'time' (in general) and 'weather'. '¿Qué tiempo hace?' (What's the weather like?). For clock time, use 'hora': '¿Qué hora es?' (What time is it?).
gente is grammatically singular in Spanish: 'La gente es buena' (People are good). For 'people' as a countable group, use personas: 'hay tres personas' (there are three people).

levels.front: There are a lot of people here.
I want to travel the world.

levels.grammarNote: vez appears in many key expressions: una vez (once), a veces (sometimes), muchas veces (often), de vez en cuando (from time to time), otra vez (again), cada vez más (more and more).
'trabajo' is the noun (work/job) and also the yo form of 'trabajar' (I work). 'buscar trabajo' = to look for a job. 'ir al trabajo' = to go to work.
'¿Cuál es tu nombre?' = What's your name? (more formal). The natural alternative: '¿Cómo te llamas?' (What do you call yourself?). 'a nombre de' = in the name of.
amigo (male friend), amiga (female friend). In mixed groups, amigos is used. 'amigo mío' = a friend of mine. 'mejor amigo' = best friend.

levels.front: He's a good friend of mine.

levels.grammarNote: bueno shortens to buen before masculine singular nouns: 'un buen día' (a good day). Feminine stays: 'una buena idea'. 'Buenas' alone is a casual greeting at any hour.
malo shortens to mal before masculine singular nouns: 'un mal día' (a bad day). 'estar mal' = to feel bad or to be wrong. 'qué mala suerte' = what bad luck.
grande shortens to gran before any noun (masc. or fem.): 'un gran hombre' (a great man), 'una gran idea' (a great idea). Gran often suggests greatness, not just physical size.
nuevo usually goes after the noun: 'un teléfono nuevo'. Before the noun it can imply 'another/different': 'un nuevo intento' (a new/fresh attempt).
bien (adverb) modifies verbs and adjectives: 'hablas bien' (you speak well). bueno (adjective) describes nouns: 'un buen libro'. 'Está bien' = it's okay/that's fine.
mal is the adverb form of malo. 'me siento mal' (I feel bad), 'dormir mal' (to sleep badly), 'salió mal' (it went wrong). Also: 'no está mal' = not bad.
todo agrees with the noun: todo el día (all day), toda la noche (all night), todos los días (every day), todas las semanas (every week). 'todo' alone = everything.
Unlike English, Spanish does NOT use an article before otro: say 'otro café' (not 'un otro café'). 'otra vez' = again. 'el uno al otro' = each other.

mucho agrees with the noun when used as an adjective: mucho trabajo (a lot of work), mucha gente (a lot of people), muchos años (many years). As an adverb it's invariable: 'habla mucho' (he talks a lot).

levels.front: I have a lot to do.

levels.grammarNote: un poco de + noun = a little of: 'un poco de agua' (a little water). poco as adjective = few: 'poca gente' (few people). 'poco a poco' = little by little.
sí (with accent) = yes. si (no accent) = if. 'Sí, claro' = Yes, of course. '¿Sí?' by itself = really? / is that so? (expressing surprise or asking for confirmation).
no goes directly before the verb: 'no como carne' (I don't eat meat). Double negatives are correct in Spanish: 'no tengo nada' (I don't have anything / I have nothing).
'más de' before numbers (más de cinco = more than five). 'más que' in other comparisons (más alto que yo = taller than me). 'más o menos' = more or less / so-so.

levels.front: I need a little more time.
levels.back: Necesito un poco más de tiempo.

levels.grammarNote: algo = something (in positive sentences) or anything (in questions). 'alguien' = someone. In negative sentences use 'nada' (nothing) and 'nadie' (nobody).

levels.front: I want to tell you something.

levels.grammarNote: nada = nothing. With double negation: 'no tengo nada' (I don't have anything/I have nothing). 'de nada' = you're welcome. 'nada más' = nothing else / that's all.
nunca before the verb = never: 'nunca llego tarde'. After the verb, add no: 'no llego tarde nunca'. Both are correct. Spanish double negatives are grammatical.
ya is extremely versatile: 'ya terminé' (I already finished), '¡ya voy!' (I'm coming!), 'ya no' (not anymore), '¡ya!' (okay! / got it!), 'ya que' (since/because). You'll hear it constantly.
dormir stem-changes o→ue: yo duermo, tú duermes, él duerme. nosotros dormimos is regular. 'dormir bien/mal' = to sleep well/badly.

levels.front: How many hours did you sleep?

levels.grammarNote: 'tomar' is often used instead of beber for drinks in Latin America: '¿Quieres tomar algo?' (Do you want to drink something?). beber is more common in Spain.

levels.front: Do you want something to drink?

levels.grammarNote: pensar stem-changes e→ie: yo pienso, tú piensas, él piensa. 'pensar en' = to think about: 'pienso en ti' (I'm thinking about you). 'pensar + infinitive' = to plan to: 'pienso ir' (I plan to go).

levels.front: What do you think about it?

levels.grammarNote: sentir (e→ie: siento) = to feel or sense something. sentirse = to feel (describing your own state): '¿Cómo te sientes?' (How do you feel?). 'Lo siento' = I'm sorry (I feel it).
encontrar stem-changes o→ue: encuentro, encuentras, encuentra. 'encontrar' = to find something. 'encontrarse con' = to run into / meet up with someone.
seguir stem-changes e→i: yo sigo, tú sigues. 'seguir + gerund' = to keep doing something: 'sigue hablando' (keep talking). 'sigue recto' = go straight.
volver stem-changes o→ue: vuelvo, vuelves, vuelve. 'volver a + infinitive' = to do something again: 'vuelvo a intentarlo' (I'll try again). 'volver en sí' = to come to / regain consciousness.
salir is irregular in yo: salgo. 'salir de' = to leave from. 'salir a' = to go out to. '¿Salimos a comer?' = Shall we go out to eat?

levels.front: What time do you finish work?

levels.grammarNote: poner is irregular in yo: pongo. 'poner la mesa' = to set the table. 'ponerse' = to put on (clothing) or to become: 'me puse nervioso' (I got nervous).
traer is irregular in yo: traigo. Preterite is irregular: traje, trajiste, trajo. 'traer' = to bring (toward speaker). Compare: llevar = to take (away from speaker).
'parece que' = it seems that. 'me parece que' = it seems to me that / I think. 'me parece bien' = it seems fine to me / I'm okay with it. Irregular yo: parezco.

levels.front: It seems like it's going to rain.

levels.grammarNote: empezar stem-changes e→ie: yo empiezo, tú empiezas. 'empezar a + infinitive' = to start doing something: 'empecé a estudiar' (I started studying). Spelling change in preterite: empecé (not empezé).
'terminar de + infinitive' = to finish doing something: '¿terminaste de comer?' (did you finish eating?). 'al final' = in the end. 'por fin' = finally.
'¿Qué hora es?' = What time is it? 'Son las tres' = It's three o'clock. 'a las ocho' = at eight o'clock. Compare: tiempo = time in general, hora = clock time / hour.
'en lugar de' = instead of. 'tener lugar' = to take place. Compare: sitio (spot/place, more informal), lugar (place, slightly formal).

levels.front: Do you know a good place to eat?
levels.literal: Know [you] a good place to eat?

levels.grammarNote: 'la mayor parte de' = most of. 'de parte de' = on behalf of. 'por una parte... por otra parte' = on one hand... on the other hand. Note: parte is feminine.
problema is masculine despite ending in -a: el problema, un problema (not la problema). Other masculine -a words: el tema (topic), el sistema (system), el programa (program).
'hacer una pregunta' = to ask a question (not 'pedir' or 'preguntar una pregunta'). 'preguntar' (the verb) = to ask. 'pregunta' (the noun) = question.

levels.front: Can I ask you a question?

levels.grammarNote: 'el camino a' = the way to / road to. 'en el camino' = on the way. 'camino a casa' = on the way home. Famous: 'El Camino de Santiago' (the pilgrimage route across Spain).

levels.front: Are we going the right way?

levels.grammarNote: 'lo mismo' = the same (thing): 'Quiero lo mismo' (I want the same). 'yo mismo' = myself, 'ella misma' = herself. 'al mismo tiempo' = at the same time.

levels.back: Lo mismo para mí, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: solo (adjective) = alone: 'estoy solo' (I'm alone). The adverb solo = only (synonymous with solamente) — formerly written sólo with an accent, but the accent is now optional per the RAE.
primero shortens to primer before masculine singular nouns: 'el primer día' (the first day), but 'la primera vez' (the first time).

levels.front: This is my first time in Mexico.
levels.back: Es mi primera vez en México.

levels.grammarNote: 'por último' = lastly / finally. 'lo último' = the latest thing. 'a última hora' = at the last minute.
mejor = better (comparative) and best (superlative) of bueno. 'es mejor que' = it's better than. 'lo mejor' = the best thing. 'mejor dicho' = or rather / to be more precise.

levels.front: This is the best coffee I've had.
levels.back: Es el mejor café que he tomado.
levels.literal: [It] is the best coffee that [I]-have had.

levels.grammarNote: peor = worse (comparative) and worst (superlative) of malo. 'es peor que' = it's worse than. 'lo peor' = the worst thing. 'de mal en peor' = from bad to worse.
cada is invariable (doesn't change for gender or number). 'cada día' (every day), 'cada vez más' (more and more), 'cada vez menos' (less and less), 'cada uno' (each one).
'antes de + infinitive' = before doing something: 'llámame antes de llegar' (call me before you arrive). 'antes de que + subjunctive' = before: 'antes de que llegues' (before you arrive).
'después de + infinitive' = after doing something: 'después de comer' (after eating). 'después de que + subjunctive' = after: 'después de que llegues' (after you arrive).

levels.back: Nos vemos después de la reunión.

levels.grammarNote: aunque + indicative = although (factual): 'aunque es tarde, sigo aquí' (although it's late, I'm still here). aunque + subjunctive = even if (hypothetical): 'aunque llueva, voy' (even if it rains, I'll go).

levels.front: I'll go even if it rains.

levels.grammarNote: Both quizás and tal vez mean 'maybe'. They can take the indicative (more certain) or subjunctive (more doubtful): 'tal vez viene' (maybe he's coming) vs 'tal vez venga' (maybe he'll come — uncertain).

levels.front: It was hard. However, I did it.
levels.back: Fue difícil. Sin embargo, lo hice.
A veces voy al trabajo a pie.
levels.literal: At times [I]-go to-the work on foot.

levels.grammarNote: 'por supuesto' = of course (formal/emphatic). Casual alternatives: '¡claro!', '¡claro que sí!', '¡cómo no!'. All are very common.
'de nada' (of nothing) is the standard response to gracias. Alternatives: 'no hay de qué' (don't mention it), 'con gusto' (with pleasure — common in Latin America), 'a ti' (thank you — casual).

ojalá comes from Arabic ('inshallah'). It always expresses a wish and typically triggers the subjunctive: 'ojalá venga' (I hope he comes), 'ojalá pudiera' (I wish I could). It's one of the most expressive words in Spanish.

'a ver' is one of the most common fillers in spoken Spanish, like 'let me see', 'well...', or 'hmm'. It can also mean 'let's see' when looking at something, or soften a command: 'a ver, cuéntame' (okay, tell me).

levels.front: Let me see... I think so.

levels.grammarNote: Use ¿cuál? before 'es' and 'son': '¿Cuál es tu nombre?' (What's your name?). Use ¿qué? before nouns: '¿Qué libro?' (Which book?). This trips up many learners — cuál is 'which' but translates as 'what' when followed by a verb.
cuánto agrees with the noun: '¿cuánto dinero?' (how much money?), '¿cuántas personas?' (how many people?). As an adverb, invariable: '¿cuánto cuesta?' (how much does it cost?).
'¿Cómo?' on its own = 'What?' or 'Pardon?' when you didn't catch what someone said — very natural to use. '¿Cómo te fue?' = How did it go? '¿Cómo que no?' = What do you mean, no?
-ar verb: hablo, hablas, habla, hablamos, hablan. Most learners' first verb.
escuchar = active listening; oír = passive hearing. '¿Me escuchas?' = Are you listening?
-er verb: como, comes, come, comemos, comen. '¿Ya comiste?' = Did you eat yet?
-ir verb. Preterite: escribí, escribiste, escribió. 'te escribo' = I'll write to you.
Preterite: leí, leíste, leyó, leyeron ('leyó' not 'leió').
'Pagar en efectivo' (cash) or 'con tarjeta' (by card). Preterite spelling: pagué.
Stem-changes e→ie: cierro, cierras, cierra. nosotros cerramos is regular.
Stem-changes e→ie: entiendo, entiendes, entiende. 'No entiendo' is one of the most useful phrases.

levels.front: I don't understand. Can you repeat?

levels.grammarNote: 'Aprender a + infinitive' = to learn to do something: 'aprendo a conducir' (I'm learning to drive).
enseñar = to teach AND to show. '¿Me enseñas?' = Can you show/teach me?
'Ayudar a + infinitive' = help to do: 'te ayudo a hacerlo' (I'll help you do it).
Takes personal 'a' with people: 'te busco' (I'll look for you). Preterite: busqué.
esperar = to wait AND to hope. 'Espero que + subjunctive' = I hope that.
llamarse = to be called: '¿Cómo te llamas?' (What's your name? — lit: What do you call yourself?)
preguntar = ask a question. pedir = ask for (request). Do not confuse these two!
Also: responder (more formal). 'Contestar el teléfono' = to answer the phone.
mirar = to look at (active). ver = to see. 'Mira' on its own = Hey, look (to get attention).

levels.front: Look at me when I talk to you.

levels.grammarNote: tocar = to touch AND to play an instrument. jugar = to play a game/sport. 'Toca la guitarra' = he plays guitar.
Stem-changes u→ue: juego, juegas, juega. 'Jugar a' + sport. Contrast: tocar = to play an instrument.
ganar = to win a game OR to earn money. '¿Cuánto ganas?' = How much do you earn?
Stem-changes e→ie: pierdo, pierdes, pierde. 'Perderse' = to get lost.
Stem-changes o→ue: recuerdo. Also: 'acordarse de' (reflexive): 'me acuerdo de ti' (I remember you).
'Olvidarse de' (reflexive, very common): 'se me olvidó' = I forgot (lit: it forgot itself to me).
Two key meanings: to leave (something) and to let/allow. 'Déjame en paz' = leave me alone.
intentar = to try/attempt. probar = to try/taste. 'Lo intenté' = I tried.
Stem-changes e→i: consigo. = to manage to get. '¿Lo conseguiste?' = Did you manage it?

levels.front: Did you manage to do it?

levels.grammarNote: llevar = to carry, to take someone, to wear. 'Te llevo' = I'll give you a ride. Also 'llevar + time' = to have been.
sacar = take out, withdraw, get a photo. 'Sacar fotos' = take photos. Preterite: saqué.
'Entrar en' (Spain) or 'entrar a' (Latin America). '¡Entra!' = Come in!
subir = go up, get on (bus/train), climb, upload. Opposite: bajar.
bajar = go down, get off transport, download. 'Baja el volumen' = turn down the volume.

levels.front: Get off at the next stop.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tomar' covers: take the bus, make a decision, have a drink. Very versatile in Latin America.
conocer = know people/places personally. Irregular yo: conozco. Compare: saber = know facts/how-to.
'Cambiar de idea' = change one's mind. '¿Dónde cambio dinero?' = Where can I exchange money?
Irregular yo: crezco. 'Crecer' = grow physically or develop. 'Cuando crezca' = when I grow up.
Irregular yo: caigo. 'Caerse' = fall down. 'Se me cayó' = I dropped it. 'Caer bien/mal' = to like/dislike someone.
Irregular yo: nazco. Mostly used in past. 'Nací en México' = I was born in Mexico.
Stem-changes o→ue: muero. 'Me muero de hambre' = I'm starving (lit: dying of hunger).

levels.front: I need to clean the house.

levels.grammarNote: 'Prepararse' = to get oneself ready. '¿Ya te preparaste?' = Are you ready yet?

levels.front: I need to rest a bit.

levels.grammarNote: Preterite spelling: expliqué. '¿Me lo explicas?' = Can you explain it to me?
Stem-changes e→i: pido, pides, pide. = to request or order food. Different from preguntar (ask a question)!

levels.front: What are you going to order?

levels.grammarNote: Plural 'padres' = parents (not just fathers). 'mis padres' = my parents.

levels.front: How many children do you have?

levels.grammarNote: 'Hermanos' (plural) = brothers OR siblings (mixed group). '¿Tienes hermanos?' = Do you have siblings?
'Abuelos' = grandparents. 'mis abuelos' = my grandparents.
In Spain informal speech: tío/tía = dude/girl (like 'mate'). Context makes it clear!
Also: marido (husband), mujer (wife, in context). 'Mi mujer' = my wife.
novio/novia can also mean fiancé/fiancée or groom/bride depending on context.
'Niños' = children (mixed). 'De niño/a' = as a child.

levels.front: When I was a child, I...

levels.grammarNote: 'Los jóvenes' = young people. 'joven' can be adjective or noun.

levels.front: Young people use social media a lot.
levels.back: Los jóvenes usan mucho las redes sociales.

levels.front: This movie is for adults only.
levels.back: Esta película es solo para adultos.

levels.grammarNote: Also: 'una persona mayor'. In Latin America, 'señor/señora' is a respectful address.

levels.front: Please give up your seat for elderly people.
levels.back: Por favor, ceda su asiento a los ancianos.

levels.grammarNote: Grammatically singular: 'la familia es lo más importante'.
pareja = couple (romantic) OR partner (gender-neutral). 'Mi pareja' avoids specifying gender.

levels.front: I'm going to a wedding this Saturday.
levels.back: Voy a una boda este sábado.

levels.grammarNote: 'Mi amor' = my love (endearment). 'Amor a primera vista' = love at first sight.

levels.front: I fell in love with her.
Our friendship means a lot to me.
levels.back: Nuestra amistad significa mucho para mí.

levels.grammarNote: compañero de trabajo = coworker; compañero de clase = classmate; compañero de cuarto = roommate.
In Spain informal: colega = friend/buddy (like 'mate'). 'Colega de trabajo' = work colleague.

levels.front: A colleague told me about it.
They used to be friends but now they're enemies.
levels.back: Antes eran amigos, ahora son enemigos.

levels.grammarNote: conocido = someone you know but aren't close to. 'Un conocido mío' = an acquaintance of mine.
matrimonio = the institution OR a married couple. 'Llevan 20 años de matrimonio' = they've been married 20 years.

levels.front: She has been a widow for ten years.

levels.grammarNote: 'Con cariño' = with love (letter sign-off). As endearment: darling/honey.
'Un abrazo' at the end of a message = best wishes / take care (like signing off a letter).
'Un beso' / 'besos' = casual letter sign-off (hugs and kisses). Also: greeting kiss on the cheek.
'Apoyar' = to support (verb). 'Cuenta con mi apoyo' = you have my support.
'De confianza' = trustworthy. 'Con confianza' = freely/confidently.
'Respetar' = to respect (verb). 'Faltarle el respeto a alguien' = to be disrespectful to someone.
'Discutir' = to argue (false friend — not 'to discuss'!). 'Tener una pelea' = to have a fight.
'Llevarse bien con alguien' = to get along well. Opposite: 'llevarse mal'.
Spain: echar de menos. Latin America: extrañar. Both = to miss someone/something.
'Cuidar a alguien' = to look after someone. '¡Cuídate!' = Take care! (common goodbye).

levels.front: She takes care of her mother.

levels.grammarNote: 'Bien criado' = well-raised/well-mannered. 'Criarse' = to grow up.
gemelos = identical twins. mellizos = fraternal twins (different eggs).

levels.front: They're twins — they look exactly alike.
levels.back: Son gemelos — se parecen exactamente igual.

levels.grammarNote: 'Suegros' = in-laws (parents of spouse).

levels.front: I get along well with my in-laws.
levels.back: Me llevo bien con mis suegros.

levels.grammarNote: 'Padrinos' = godparents. Also the wedding equivalent of best man/maid of honor in Spanish culture.
Careful! 'Pariente' = relative, NOT 'parent' (false friend). 'Padres' = parents.

levels.front: I have a half-sister in Barcelona.
levels.back: Tengo una media hermana en Barcelona.

levels.grammarNote: 'Me duele la cabeza' = I have a headache (lit: the head hurts me). 'Perder la cabeza' = to lose one's mind.
'Cara a cara' = face to face. 'Tener cara de cansado' = to look tired.
'¡Ojo!' = Watch out! Very useful warning. 'Echar un ojo' = to keep an eye on.
'No abras la boca' = don't say a word. 'Boca abajo' = face down.
oreja = outer ear. oído = inner ear / sense of hearing. 'Tener buen oído' = to have a good ear for music.
cuello = neck AND collar of a shirt. 'Me duele el cuello' = I have a stiff neck.
'Encoger los hombros' = to shrug.

levels.front: I slept on my shoulder wrong.

levels.grammarNote: 'Con los brazos abiertos' = with open arms.
mano is feminine despite -o ending: la mano. 'A mano' = by hand. 'Manos a la obra' = let's get to work.

levels.back: Lávate las manos antes de comer.

levels.grammarNote: dedo = finger or toe. 'Dedo del pie' = toe. 'Dedo gordo' = big toe / thumb.
'Tomárselo a pecho' = to take it to heart.
'Dar la espalda' = to turn one's back on someone.
'Estómago' = anatomical. 'Barriga' = informal belly. 'Me duele el estómago' = my stomach hurts.
'De rodillas' = on one's knees.

levels.front: My knee hurts when I run.
levels.back: Me duele la rodilla cuando corro.

levels.grammarNote: 'A pie' = on foot. 'De pie' = standing.
'De corazón' = sincerely / from the heart.

levels.front: From the bottom of my heart.

levels.grammarNote: 'Donar sangre' = to donate blood. 'A sangre fría' = in cold blood.
'Estar en los huesos' = to be all skin and bones.

levels.front: You need to stretch your muscles.

levels.grammarNote: 'Piel de gallina' = goosebumps (lit: chicken skin).
pelo = common/informal. cabello = literary. 'Tomarte el pelo' = to pull your leg.
'Salud' also = Cheers! (toast) and Bless you! (sneeze). Three uses, one word.
'Estar enfermo/a' = to be sick. 'Ponerse enfermo/a' = to get sick.
'Me duele (la cabeza)' = I have a headache. 'Dolores de crecimiento' = growing pains.
'Tener fiebre' = to have a fever. 'Bajarle la fiebre' = to bring down the fever.
'Medicina' = medicine (field) or medication. 'Medicamento' = medication specifically.

levels.back: Toma esto dos veces al día.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ir al médico' = to go to the doctor. 'Doctor/a' is also widely used.

levels.front: I need to see a doctor.

levels.grammarNote: Pharmacists in Spain/LatAm can give basic medical advice for minor issues without prescription.

levels.front: The nurse gave me an injection.
levels.back: La enfermera me puso una inyección.
levels.literal: The nurse to-me put an injection.

levels.grammarNote: 'Pedir cita' = to make an appointment.

levels.front: I have a doctor's appointment tomorrow.
Take one pill with each meal.
levels.back: Toma una pastilla con cada comida.

levels.grammarNote: 'Poner una inyección' = to give an injection.
'Ser alérgico/a a' = to be allergic to.
'Tener gripe' = to have the flu. 'Gripe estacional' = seasonal flu.
'Estar resfriado/a' = to have a cold. Spain: coger un resfriado. LatAm: agarrar un resfriado.

levels.front: I think I'm catching a cold.
levels.back: Creo que estoy agarrando un resfriado.

levels.grammarNote: 'Herir' = to wound/injure. 'Herida abierta' = open wound.
'Estar cansado/a' = to be tired. 'Muerto de cansancio' = dead tired.
sueño = sleepiness ('tengo sueño' = I'm sleepy) AND dream ('tuve un sueño'). 'El sueño americano' = the American Dream.
Feminine but uses 'el' in singular: 'el hambre'. 'Tener hambre' = to be hungry.
'Tener sed' = to be thirsty.
Warning! embarazada ≠ embarrassed. It means pregnant! 'Estar embarazada' = to be pregnant.
'Curarse' = to get better. '¡Que te mejores!' = Get well soon!
'Operarse' = to have surgery. 'Le operaron del corazón' = he had heart surgery.

levels.front: She had an operation last week.

levels.grammarNote: receta = recipe AND prescription. Context makes it clear. 'Receta sin receta' = over-the-counter.
'Hacer ejercicio' = to exercise.
'Estar a dieta' = to be on a diet. 'Dieta mediterránea' = Mediterranean diet.
peso = weight AND the currency in many LatAm countries. 'Perder/ganar peso' = to lose/gain weight.
comida = food in general AND the main midday meal. 'La comida está lista' = The food is ready.

levels.front: What do you want to drink?

levels.grammarNote: Feminine but takes 'el' in singular: 'el agua'. Plural: las aguas. 'Agua del grifo' = tap water.

levels.back: Un vaso de agua, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Café con leche' = coffee with milk (very common in Spain). 'Leche entera' = whole milk.
'Café solo' = black coffee (Spain). 'Café cortado' = espresso with a drop of milk. 'Café de olla' = Mexican spiced coffee.

levels.back: Voy a pedir un café solo.

levels.grammarNote: 'Té verde' = green tea. 'Té con limón' = tea with lemon. '¿Me pones un té?' = Can you get me a tea?

levels.back: Un té con leche, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: jugo = Latin America. zumo = Spain. 'Jugo de naranja' = orange juice.

levels.back: Un jugo de naranja natural, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Vino tinto' = red wine. 'Vino blanco' = white wine. 'Vino rosado' = rosé. 'Cata de vinos' = wine tasting.

levels.front: A glass of red wine, please.
levels.back: Una copa de vino tinto, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cerveza de barril' = draught beer. 'Una caña' (Spain) = a small draught beer. 'Una chela' (Mexico) = a beer.
'Pan de molde' = sliced bread. 'Panecillo' = bread roll. 'Pan tostado' = toast.
'Arroz con leche' = rice pudding. 'Arroz a la cubana' = Cuban-style rice with fried egg and tomato.
Pasta is widely eaten across Latin America and Spain. 'Pasta al dente' is a common instruction.

levels.front: How long does the pasta cook?

levels.grammarNote: 'Sopa de verduras' = vegetable soup. 'Gazpacho' = famous Spanish cold tomato soup.
'Ensalada mixta' = mixed salad (very common on Spanish menus: lettuce, tomato, onion, tuna, olives, egg).
'Carne de res' = beef (LatAm). 'Ternera' = veal/beef (Spain). 'Carne de cerdo' = pork.
pescado = fish (as food). pez = fish (alive, in water).
'Huevo frito' = fried egg. 'Huevo revuelto' = scrambled egg. 'Huevo duro' = hard-boiled egg.
'Queso manchego' = famous Spanish sheep cheese. 'Queso fundido' = melted cheese.
'Echar sal' = to add salt. 'Sin sal' = without salt / unsalted. 'Le falta sal' = it needs more salt.

levels.front: It needs a bit more salt.
levels.back: Le falta un poco de sal.
levels.literal: To-it lacks a little of salt.

levels.grammarNote: azúcar can be masculine or feminine in Spanish — both 'el' and 'la' are used. 'Sin azúcar' = sugar-free.
'Aceite de oliva' = olive oil (central to Spanish and Mediterranean cooking). 'Aceite de girasol' = sunflower oil.

levels.front: Everything is cooked in olive oil here.
levels.back: Aquí todo se cocina con aceite de oliva.

levels.grammarNote: fruta = fruit (collective/general). frutas = various fruits. 'Fruta de temporada' = seasonal fruit.
'Verduras' = green vegetables (also used broadly). 'Vegetales' = vegetables (more common in LatAm).
'Manzana' also = city block in Latin America. 'A dos manzanas' = two blocks away.
naranja as an adjective = the color orange. 'Naranja natural' = freshly squeezed orange.
plátano (Spain/LatAm). banana (many LatAm countries). In Spain 'plátano' can also mean plantain — context matters.

levels.front: I eat a banana every morning.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tomate triturado' = crushed tomatoes. 'Tomate frito' = tomato sauce (Spain). Essential in Spanish cuisine!

levels.front: A salad with lettuce and tomato.
levels.back: Una ensalada con lechuga y tomate.

levels.grammarNote: 'Desayunar' = to have breakfast. '¿Ya desayunaste?' = Have you had breakfast? Spanish breakfast is often light: coffee + toast or churros.

levels.front: What do you have for breakfast?

levels.grammarNote: In Spain: 'la comida' = the main midday meal. 'Almuerzo' is common in LatAm. Spaniards eat lunch 2–4pm.

levels.front: Lunch is the main meal in Spain.
levels.back: En España, la comida es la comida principal.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cenar' = to have dinner. In Spain, dinner is often late (9–10pm).

levels.front: We eat dinner at nine in Spain.
levels.back: En España cenamos a las nueve.

levels.grammarNote: merienda = mid-afternoon snack (around 5–6pm), a Spanish tradition especially for children.

levels.front: The kids want their afternoon snack.

levels.grammarNote: plato = the plate AND a dish/course on a menu. 'El plato del día' = today's special. 'Plato fuerte' = main course.

levels.literal: Which is the plate of the day?

levels.grammarNote: taza = cup (with a handle, like for coffee/tea). vaso = glass (for cold drinks). 'Una taza de café' = a cup of coffee.

levels.back: Una taza de café, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: vaso = drinking glass (without stem). copa = stemmed glass (wine, champagne). 'Un vaso de agua' = a glass of water.

levels.back: Un vaso de agua, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tenedor de postre' = dessert fork.

levels.front: Can I have a clean fork?

levels.grammarNote: 'Cuchara' = spoon. 'Cucharilla / cucharita' = teaspoon.

levels.front: I need a spoon for the soup.
levels.back: Necesito una cuchara para la sopa.

levels.grammarNote: cocina = kitchen (room) AND cooking/cuisine. 'La cocina española' = Spanish cuisine.
receta = recipe AND prescription (medical). 'Seguir una receta' = to follow a recipe.

levels.front: Can you give me the recipe?

levels.grammarNote: 'Saber' = to know AND to taste ('¿A qué sabe?' = What does it taste like?). 'Sabor' = the flavor.
rico = tasty/rich (food context). '¡Qué rico!' = How delicious! 'delicioso' = delicious (more formal).
'Dulce' as noun = sweet/candy. 'Más dulce que el azúcar' = sweeter than sugar. 'Dulces' = sweets/candy.
'Picante' = spicy (from chili etc). 'Caliente' = hot (temperature). Don't confuse them!
'Patatas fritas' = fries/chips. 'Huevo frito' = fried egg. 'Pescado frito' = fried fish.
'Crudo' = raw (food). 'En crudo' = raw/unprocessed. Note: 'crudeza' = harshness/bluntness.

levels.back: La carne está cruda por dentro.

levels.grammarNote: 'Bien cocido' = well done (meat). 'Poco cocido' = rare. 'Cocido madrileño' = famous Madrid stew.
'En casa' = at home. 'A casa' = home(ward). 'Casa de campo' = country house. 'Casa propia' = one's own home.
piso (Spain) = apartment/flat. piso also = floor (level). 'Primer piso' = first floor.

levels.front: I rent an apartment in the city center.
levels.back: Alquilo un piso en el centro.

levels.grammarNote: habitación = room (formal). cuarto = room (informal). 'Cuarto de baño' = bathroom.
salón (Spain) = living room. sala de estar (LatAm). 'Sala' also = waiting room / hall.

levels.front: We watch TV in the living room.
levels.back: Vemos la tele en el salón.

levels.front: The dining room fits ten people.

levels.grammarNote: dormitorio (formal). cuarto (informal). 'Habitación' also used. 'Suite' = luxury bedroom.
baño = bathroom AND bath. 'Cuarto de baño' (full bathroom). '¿Dónde está el baño?' = very useful question!
'Jardín trasero' = backyard. 'Jardín botánico' = botanical garden.

levels.front: I love working in the garden.
levels.back: Me encanta trabajar en el jardín.

levels.grammarNote: 'Puerta principal' = main/front door. 'Llamar a la puerta' = to knock on the door. 'Puerta trasera' = back door.
'Abrir / cerrar la ventana' = to open/close the window. 'Ventana al mundo' = window to the world (fig.).
escalera = staircase AND ladder. 'Escaleras mecánicas' = escalator. 'Subir las escaleras' = to go up the stairs.

levels.front: Take the stairs, not the elevator.
levels.back: Sube las escaleras, no el ascensor.

levels.grammarNote: suelo = floor (ground level). piso = floor (storey) AND floor surface (LatAm). 'En el suelo' = on the ground/floor.
techo = ceiling (inside) AND roof (outside). 'Bajo el mismo techo' = under the same roof.
pared = interior wall. muro = exterior/thick wall. 'Hablar con la pared' = to talk to a wall (fig.).

levels.front: Hang the picture on that wall.
levels.back: Cuelga el cuadro en esa pared.
levels.literal: Hang the painting on that wall.

levels.grammarNote: 'Muebles' (plural) = furniture. 'Un mueble' = a piece of furniture. 'Tienda de muebles' = furniture store.

levels.front: We need to buy new furniture.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hacer la cama' = to make the bed. 'Cama doble' = double bed. 'Cama individual' = single bed. 'Quedarse en cama' = to stay in bed.

levels.front: I didn't make the bed today.

levels.grammarNote: silla = chair (with back). sillón = armchair. taburete = stool. 'Tomar asiento' = to take a seat.
'Poner la mesa' = to set the table. 'Quitar la mesa' = to clear the table. 'Mesa de noche' = bedside table.
'Lámpara de pie' = floor lamp. 'Lámpara de techo' = ceiling light.
'Mirarse al espejo' = to look at oneself in the mirror.

levels.front: There's a big mirror in the bathroom.
levels.back: Hay un espejo grande en el baño.

levels.grammarNote: armario = wardrobe (Spain) / closet. 'Armario empotrado' = built-in wardrobe.
'Llave de la casa' = house key. 'Llave inglesa' = wrench. 'Bajo llave' = under lock and key.
'Pagar el alquiler' = to pay rent. 'De alquiler' = rental (adj). 'Alquilar' = to rent.
ropa is always singular in Spanish: 'la ropa'. 'Cambiar de ropa' = to change clothes. 'Ropa sucia' = dirty laundry.

levels.front: I need to do the laundry.

levels.grammarNote: camisa = dress shirt (with buttons). camiseta = T-shirt. 'Ponerse una camisa' = to put on a shirt.
Used in both singular and plural in Spanish. 'Pantalón vaquero / jeans' = jeans.

levels.front: That skirt looks great on you.
levels.back: Esa falda te queda muy bien.
levels.literal: That skirt to-you fits very well.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ponerse un vestido' = to put on a dress.
'Zapato de tacón' = high heel. 'Zapato plano' = flat shoe. 'Zapatillas' = sneakers/slippers.
'Ponerse el abrigo' = to put on your coat.
chaqueta = jacket (lightweight). abrigo = coat (heavy). 'Chaqueta de cuero' = leather jacket.
suéter (LatAm). jersey (Spain). 'Jersey de cuello alto' = turtleneck.
sombrero = hat (any type). 'Gorra' = cap (baseball cap). 'Sombrero de copa' = top hat.

levels.front: Put on your hat, the sun is strong.
levels.back: Ponte el sombrero, el sol es fuerte.
levels.literal: Put-yourself the hat, the sun is strong.

levels.grammarNote: gafas (Spain) / lentes or anteojos (LatAm). 'Gafas de sol' = sunglasses.
bolso = handbag/purse. 'Bolsa' = bag (shopping bag, general bag). 'Bolsillo' = pocket.

levels.front: She left her purse in the taxi.
levels.back: Dejó el bolso en el taxi.

levels.grammarNote: 'Abrocharse el cinturón de seguridad' = to fasten your seatbelt.
talla = size (clothing). '¿Qué talla usas?' = What size do you wear? 'Número' = size (shoes).
'Estar de moda' = to be in fashion/trendy. 'Pasado de moda' = old-fashioned. 'Alta moda' = high fashion.

levels.back: Ese estilo está muy de moda.

levels.grammarNote: '¿Qué llevas puesto?' = What are you wearing? llevar + article of clothing = to wear.

levels.front: I need to sweep the floor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tirar la basura' = to take out the trash. 'Cubo de basura' = trash can. 'Reciclaje' = recycling.

levels.front: To tidy up / put in order
levels.grammarNote: 'Ordenar' = to tidy. 'Arreglar' = to fix or tidy. '¡Ordena tu cuarto!' = Clean your room!
'Se fue la luz' = the power went out. 'Factura de la luz' = electricity bill.
dueño = owner. 'Propietario' = property owner. 'Casero/a' = landlord/landlady.

levels.front: The restaurant is on Fifth Avenue.
levels.back: El restaurante está en la Quinta Avenida.

levels.grammarNote: 'Plaza mayor' = main square (central feature of most Spanish cities). 'Aparcar en la plaza' = to park in the square.

levels.front: Meet me in the main square.

levels.grammarNote: 'Parque nacional' = national park. 'Parque de atracciones' = amusement park.

levels.front: Let's take a walk in the park.
levels.back: Vamos a dar un paseo por el parque.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tienda de ropa' = clothing store. 'Tienda de campaña' = tent. 'Ir de tiendas' = to go shopping.

levels.front: What time does the store close?

levels.grammarNote: 'Mercado de abastos' = food market. 'Mercado de pulgas' = flea market. Very common in LatAm and Spain.

levels.front: I buy vegetables at the market.
Is there a supermarket near here?

levels.grammarNote: banco = bank AND bench (park bench). 'Ir al banco' = to go to the bank. 'Cuenta bancaria' = bank account.

levels.front: I need to go to the bank.

levels.grammarNote: correos = post office (Spain, always plural). 'Oficina de correos' or 'correo' in LatAm. 'Echar una carta' = to mail a letter.

levels.front: I'd like to make a reservation.

levels.grammarNote: In Spain, 'bar' = a casual place for coffee, food, or drinks at any time of day. Different from AngloAmerican bars.

levels.front: Shall we go for a coffee at the bar?
The museum is free on Sundays.
levels.back: El museo es gratis los domingos.

levels.grammarNote: escuela = school (general). colegio = school (K-12 in LatAm/Spain). 'Colegio' in Spain ≠ college/university!

levels.front: My children go to school nearby.
levels.back: Mis hijos van al colegio cerca.

levels.grammarNote: 'La uni' = informal for university. 'Ir a la universidad' = to go to university.
biblioteca = library. librería = bookstore (false friend!). Don't confuse them.

levels.front: I'm going to the library to study.
levels.back: Voy a la biblioteca a estudiar.

levels.grammarNote: estación = station AND season (of the year). Context makes it clear. 'Estación de tren/autobús' = train/bus station.

levels.literal: At what distance is the airport?

levels.grammarNote: puerto = port/harbor AND mountain pass. 'Puerto deportivo' = marina.

levels.front: The ship leaves from the port.

levels.grammarNote: autobús (Spain). camión (Mexico). micro (Chile/Peru). bus (widely used). 'Parada de autobús' = bus stop.
'Tren de alta velocidad' = high-speed train (AVE in Spain). 'Perder el tren' = to miss the train.
metro = subway/underground. 'Coger el metro' = to take the subway (Spain). 'Agarrar el metro' (LatAm).

levels.back: Toma el metro hasta la línea 2.

levels.grammarNote: 'Pedir un taxi' = to call/order a taxi. 'Taxi libre' = free/available taxi (sign on the car).

levels.front: Can you call a taxi for me?

levels.grammarNote: 'Coger un vuelo' = to take a flight. 'Vuelo de ida y vuelta' = round-trip flight. 'Retraso del vuelo' = flight delay.

levels.back: Mi vuelo sale a las seis.

levels.grammarNote: 'Barco' = boat/ship (general). 'Ferry' = ferry. 'Crucero' = cruise ship. 'Lancha' = small motorboat.

levels.front: The ferry crosses in two hours.
levels.back: El ferry cruza en dos horas.

levels.grammarNote: coche (Spain). carro (most of LatAm). auto (Argentina/Uruguay). 'Alquilar un coche' = to rent a car.
'Bici' = informal. 'Carril bici' = bike lane. 'Montar en bici' = to ride a bike.

levels.front: I go to work by bike.

levels.grammarNote: 'Moto' = short for motocicleta. 'Ir en moto' = to go by motorcycle.
'Gasolina sin plomo' = unleaded petrol. 'Gasolinera' = gas station. 'Llenar el depósito' = to fill up the tank.
parada = stop (bus/train stop). 'Próxima parada' = next stop. 'Parada de taxi' = taxi stand.

levels.front: Get off at the third stop.

levels.grammarNote: billete (Spain) = ticket AND banknote. boleto (LatAm) = ticket. 'Sacar un billete' = to buy a ticket.

levels.back: Dos billetes para Barcelona, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Solo ida' = one way. 'Ida y vuelta' = round trip. 'Billete de ida y vuelta' = return ticket.

levels.back: Un billete de ida y vuelta, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hacer la maleta' = to pack one's suitcase. 'Maleta de mano' = carry-on bag. 'Facturar la maleta' = to check a bag.

levels.back: Mi pasaporte caduca el año que viene.
levels.literal: My passport expires the year that comes.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cruzar la frontera' = to cross the border. 'Control de fronteras' = border control.
dirección = address AND direction AND management. '¿Cuál es tu dirección?' = What's your address?
'A la derecha' = to the right. 'Girar a la derecha' = to turn right. 'Mano derecha' = right hand.

levels.back: Gira a la derecha en la esquina.
levels.literal: Turn to the right at the corner.

levels.grammarNote: 'A la izquierda' = to the left. 'Girar a la izquierda' = to turn left.

levels.front: Turn left after the traffic light.
levels.back: Gira a la izquierda después del semáforo.

levels.grammarNote: 'Sigue todo recto' = go straight ahead. 'Todo recto' = straight on. 'Recto' also = straight/direct.
'En la esquina' = at/on the corner. 'Doblar la esquina' = to turn the corner.
mapa is masculine: el mapa. 'Mapa de la ciudad' = city map. 'Mapa de carreteras' = road map.

levels.front: Do you have a map of the city?
The sun rises in the east.
levels.back: El sol sale por el este.
levels.literal: The sun exits by the east.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cerca de' = near/close to. 'Lejos de' = far from. '¿Está lejos?' = Is it far?
'Hay mucho tráfico' = there's a lot of traffic. 'Atasco / embotellamiento' = traffic jam.
aparcamiento (Spain). estacionamiento (LatAm). 'Aparcar / estacionar' = to park.
conductor (Spain/LatAm). 'Chofer' = driver (also used, often for hired drivers).
'Un segundo' = one second AND 'un segundo' = just a second (wait). 'En cuestión de segundos' = in a matter of seconds.
'Un momento' ≈ 'un minuto' = a minute/moment. 'En cinco minutos' = in five minutes.
'Son las tres y cuarto' = it's quarter past three. 'Son las tres y media' = it's half past three.

levels.front: I'll be there in half an hour.

levels.grammarNote: 'Mañana' = tomorrow AND morning. 'Esta mañana' = this morning. 'Mañana por la mañana' = tomorrow morning.
'Tarde' = afternoon/evening (noon to sunset). 'Buenas tardes' = good afternoon/evening. 'Esta tarde' = this afternoon.

levels.front: What are you doing this afternoon?

levels.grammarNote: madrugada = the early hours (midnight–6am). 'De madrugada' = in the early hours. 'Madrugar' = to get up early.

levels.front: The party went on until the early hours.
levels.back: La fiesta duró hasta la madrugada.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hoy mismo' = today itself (emphasis). 'Hoy en día' = nowadays. 'El periódico de hoy' = today's newspaper.

levels.front: I have a lot to do today.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ayer por la mañana' = yesterday morning. 'El partido de ayer' = yesterday's game. 'Ayer mismo' = just yesterday.

levels.front: I haven't eaten since the day before yesterday.
See you the day after tomorrow.

levels.grammarNote: 'Esta semana' = this week. 'La semana que viene' = next week. 'La semana pasada' = last week. 'Fin de semana' = weekend.

levels.back: Te llamo la semana que viene.

levels.grammarNote: 'Este mes' = this month. 'El mes que viene' = next month. 'El mes pasado' = last month. 'A fin de mes' = at the end of the month.

levels.front: We've been here for two months.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hace + time' = ago (with preterite) or for (with present). 'Llegué hace una hora' = I arrived an hour ago.
'El siglo XXI' = the 21st century. 'Hace siglos' = ages ago (informal hyperbole).

levels.front: This building is from the 16th century.
levels.back: Este edificio es del siglo XVI.

levels.grammarNote: Days of the week are lowercase in Spanish. 'El lunes' = on Monday. 'Los lunes' = on Mondays.

levels.front: I have a meeting on Monday.

levels.grammarNote: 'Martes 13' is unlucky in Spanish culture (like Friday the 13th in English).

levels.front: The class is on Wednesday afternoon.
levels.back: La clase es el miércoles por la tarde.
Salimos el jueves por la noche.

levels.grammarNote: '¡Por fin es viernes!' = TGIF!
'El domingo' = on Sunday. 'Los domingos' = on Sundays.
Months are lowercase in Spanish. 'En enero' = in January. 'A principios de enero' = at the beginning of January.

levels.back: Enero es el mes más frío.

levels.front: It rains a lot in March.

levels.back: Julio es el mes más caluroso.

levels.front: Many people go on holiday in August.
levels.back: Mucha gente va de vacaciones en agosto.
El colegio empieza de nuevo en septiembre.

levels.front: Halloween is at the end of October.
levels.back: Halloween es a finales de octubre.

levels.front: The Day of the Dead is in November.
levels.back: El Día de los Muertos es en noviembre.
La primavera es mi estación favorita.

levels.front: We always go to the beach in summer.
levels.back: Siempre vamos a la playa en verano.

levels.front: It snows a lot in winter.

levels.grammarNote: cumpleaños is invariable — plural is the same: 'los cumpleaños'. '¡Feliz cumpleaños!' = Happy Birthday!
'Aniversario de boda' = wedding anniversary.
vacaciones is always plural in Spanish: 'las vacaciones'. 'Estar de vacaciones' = to be on vacation.
'Día festivo / feriado' = public holiday. 'Puente' = long weekend (when a holiday falls near a weekend).
'Horario de trabajo' = work schedule. 'Horario de apertura' = opening hours.

levels.literal: Which is your schedule of opening?

levels.grammarNote: 'Plazo' = deadline or term/period. 'A plazos' = in installments. 'Fecha límite' = deadline (lit: limit date).
'Lo antes posible' = as soon as possible. 'Hasta pronto' = see you soon! 'Pronto' also = quickly (LatAm).
tarde = late (adverb) AND afternoon (noun). Context makes it clear. 'Llegar tarde' = to arrive late.
'Madrugar' = to get up very early. 'Llegar temprano' = to arrive early. 'Más temprano' = earlier.

levels.front: I got up very early today.

levels.grammarNote: 'Llegar a tiempo' = to arrive on time. 'A tiempo completo' = full-time (work). 'A tiempo parcial' = part-time.

levels.front: I see her from time to time.
levels.back: La veo de vez en cuando.

levels.front: That happened a long time ago.

levels.grammarNote: 'Llevo/llevamos + time' is more natural than desde hace. Both correct: 'Estudio desde hace tres años' = I've been studying for three years.

levels.front: I've been living here for two years.
levels.back: Vivo aquí desde hace dos años.

levels.grammarNote: 1 uno, 2 dos, 3 tres, 4 cuatro, 5 cinco, 6 seis, 7 siete, 8 ocho, 9 nueve, 10 diez.
11 once, 12 doce, 13 trece, 14 catorce, 15 quince, 16 dieciséis, 17 diecisiete, 18 dieciocho, 19 diecinueve, 20 veinte.
21 veintiuno, 30 treinta, 40 cuarenta, 50 cincuenta, 60 sesenta, 70 setenta, 80 ochenta, 90 noventa, 100 cien.
100 cien, 200 doscientos, 500 quinientos, 1000 mil, 1.000.000 un millón. Note: cien before nouns, ciento in compounds: ciento uno (101).

levels.front: The hotel costs two hundred euros per night.
levels.back: El hotel cuesta doscientos euros por noche.

levels.grammarNote: 1º primero, 2º segundo, 3º tercero, 4º cuarto, 5º quinto, 6º sexto, 7º séptimo, 8º octavo, 9º noveno, 10º décimo. Primero and tercero shorten before masculine nouns: primer, tercer.
'La mitad' = half (noun). 'A mitad de precio' = half price. 'Mitad y mitad' = half and half.
'El doble de caro' = twice as expensive. 'Una habitación doble' = a double room. 'Triple' = triple.
'¿Cuánto cuesta?' = How much does it cost? '¿A cuánto está?' = How much is it (price today)?
'Hacer un descuento' = to give a discount. 'Con descuento' = at a discount.

levels.front: Is there a discount for students?

levels.grammarNote: gratis is invariable (doesn't change). 'Entrada gratis' = free entry. '¿Es gratis?' = Is it free?
'Barato' = cheap (adj). 'Más barato' = cheaper. 'Lo más barato' = the cheapest. 'Rebajas' = sales.
'Demasiado caro' = too expensive. 'Salir caro' = to cost dearly (fig.). 'Que no me cueste caro' = I hope it doesn't cost me.

levels.back: Eso está demasiado caro para mí.

levels.grammarNote: 'Pagar en efectivo' = to pay cash. '¿Tienen cajero automático?' = Do you have an ATM?
'¿Aceptan tarjeta?' = Do you accept cards? 'Cobrar a la tarjeta' = to charge to the card.
cuenta = bill (in a restaurant) AND bank account AND story/tale. 'La cuenta, por favor' = the bill, please.
cambio = change (money returned) AND exchange rate AND gear (car). '¿Tiene cambio de 50?' = Do you have change for 50?
recibo = receipt. '¿Me da un recibo?' = Can I have a receipt? factura = invoice (more formal/official).
factura = invoice (tax document). 'Factura a nombre de' = invoice in the name of.
'Hacer la compra' = to do the grocery shopping. 'Ir de compras' = to go shopping. 'Compras online' = online shopping.

levels.front: I need to do the shopping.

levels.grammarNote: 'En venta' = for sale. 'Venta al por mayor' = wholesale. 'Ventas' = sales (department).
'Oferta especial' = special offer. 'Estar de oferta' = to be on sale/special.
'Tomar las medidas' = to take measurements. 'A medida' = custom-made. 'En cierta medida' = to a certain extent.
'¿Cuánto mides?' = How tall are you? 'Mido 1,80' = I'm 1.80m tall.
anchura = width. largo = length. 'De largo' = in length. 'De ancho' = in width.
'¿Cuántos grados hace?' = What's the temperature? 'La temperatura media' = the average temperature.
grado = degree (temperature, angle, academic). 'Grado Celsius / Fahrenheit'. 'Tengo 38 grados de fiebre' = I have a temperature of 38.
'Un kilo de' = a kilo of. 'Kilo' is the informal form used in everyday shopping.

levels.front: I'll have a kilo of tomatoes.
levels.back: Me da un kilo de tomates.
Un litro de agua, por favor.

levels.grammarNote: 'Metro' also = subway. 'Kilómetro' = km. '¿A cuántos kilómetros está?' = How many km away is it?
'Velocidad máxima' = speed limit. 'A toda velocidad' = at full speed. 'Límite de velocidad' = speed limit.
'¿Me das tu número?' = Can I have your number?

levels.front: Can I have your phone number?

levels.grammarNote: 'Más o menos' = more or less / so-so / approximately. Very common as an answer: '¿Cómo estás? — Más o menos.'
'Aproximadamente' or 'más o menos' or 'alrededor de'. All mean approximately.
'Exactamente' = exactly. 'Justo' = exactly/just. 'Eso es exactamente' = That's exactly right.

levels.back: Eso es exactamente lo que dije.

levels.grammarNote: 'En total' = in total / altogether. 'El total' = the total. '¿Cuánto es en total?' = How much is it altogether?

levels.front: How much is it in total?

levels.grammarNote: 'El promedio' = the average. 'Por encima/debajo del promedio' = above/below average.
'Número par' = even number. 'Número impar' = odd number. 'Un par de' = a couple of (≈ two).

levels.front: Is it an even or odd number?

levels.grammarNote: moneda = coin AND currency. 'Moneda extranjera' = foreign currency. 'Moneda de un euro' = a one-euro coin.
azul is the same for masculine and feminine. 'Azul marino' = navy blue. 'Azul celeste' = sky blue.

levels.front: I love the blue of the sea.
levels.back: Me encanta el azul del mar.

levels.grammarNote: verde = green (color) AND unripe. 'Estar verde' = to be inexperienced. 'Chiste verde' = dirty joke.
naranja (invariable as color adjective in Spain): 'una camisa naranja'. anaranjado (agrees with noun): 'un color anaranjado'.
morado (Spain) = purple. violeta = violet. 'Ponerse morado' = to stuff oneself (Spain slang).

levels.front: She wears a lot of purple.

levels.grammarNote: 'Mercado negro' = black market. 'Lista negra' = blacklist. 'Trabajo en negro' = off-the-books work.
'En blanco' = blank (space). 'Noche en blanco' = sleepless night. 'Quedarse en blanco' = to go blank (memory).
gris = same for masc/fem. 'Cielo gris' = grey sky. 'Un día gris' = a dull day.
marrón (Spain). café/color café (LatAm). 'Pelo castaño' = brown hair. 'Ojos marrones' = brown eyes.
rosa (invariable as adjective in Spain): 'una camisa rosa'. rosado (agrees with noun, LatAm): 'un color rosado'.
'Regla de oro' = golden rule. 'Color dorado' = golden color. 'Medalla de plata' = silver medal.
'Azul claro' = light blue. 'Verde oscuro' = dark green. 'A oscuras' = in the dark.
bonito = pretty (things and people). guapo = handsome/pretty (people). '¡Qué guapo estás!' = You look great!
'Quedarse feo' = to look bad (behavior). 'Tiempo feo' = bad weather.
joven = same for masc/fem. Plural: jóvenes. 'Hacerse mayor' = to get older.
viejo = old (things and people, can be offensive for people). mayor = older/elderly (more respectful for people). 'Un edificio viejo' = an old building.

levels.back: Es mayor de lo que parece.

levels.grammarNote: delgado = slim (neutral). flaco = thin (can imply too thin). 'Más delgado' = slimmer/thinner.
As a noun: 'el gordo' = the jackpot (lottery). 'Caerle gordo a alguien' = to find someone annoying.

levels.front: He gained a lot of weight.

levels.grammarNote: 'Alto' = tall (person) or high (thing). 'En voz alta' = aloud. 'Por todo lo alto' = in grand style.

levels.front: He's very tall for his age.
levels.back: Es muy alto para su edad.

levels.grammarNote: bajo = short (person) or low (thing or volume). 'En voz baja' = in a low voice. 'Aguas bajas' = low tide.
'Fuerte' = strong AND loud (sound) AND intense (flavor). 'Habla más fuerte' = speak louder.

levels.front: He was weakened by the illness.

levels.grammarNote: 'Rápido' as adverb: 'habla muy rápido' (he speaks very fast) — no -mente needed.
'Más despacio' = more slowly. 'Despacio' is the adverb (not 'lentamente' in everyday speech).

levels.front: The internet is very slow today.
levels.back: El internet está muy lento hoy.

levels.grammarNote: inteligente = same for masc/fem. 'Listo/a' is also common for smart/clever.
tonto = silly/foolish (mild). bobo = silly (affectionate in context). estúpido = stupid (stronger).
'Caer bien' = to make a good impression/be likeable. '¡Qué simpático!' = How friendly!
'Caer mal' = to make a bad impression. Opposite of simpático.
amable = kind/nice (more about manner/courtesy). 'Es muy amable de su parte' = That's very kind of you.
educado = polite/well-mannered (NOT just educated/learned — false friend). 'Mal educado' = rude.
grosero = crude/rude. maleducado = ill-mannered/rude. '¡Qué grosería!' = How rude!
honesto = honest. honrado = honest/honorable (more formal). 'Ser honesto' = to be honest.
'Ser valiente' = to be brave. '¡Qué valiente!' = How brave! 'Valor' = courage.
'Tranquilo' also = relax/calm down (as a command). '¡Tranquilo!' = Calm down! / Don't worry!

levels.front: Calm down, it's not that serious.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ponerse nervioso' = to get nervous. 'Tener los nervios de punta' = to have nerves on edge.

levels.front: I always get nervous before exams.
levels.back: Siempre me pongo nervioso antes de los exámenes.

levels.grammarNote: serio = serious (personality or situation). 'Estar serio' = to look serious/stern. 'En serio' = seriously / really.
gracioso = funny (person/thing). divertido = fun/entertaining. '¡Qué gracioso!' = How funny!
aburrido = boring (thing) or bored (person with estar). 'Es aburrido' = it's boring. 'Estoy aburrido' = I'm bored.
interesante = same for masc/fem. 'Hacerse el interesante' = to act interesting/play hard to get.
extraño = strange/unusual. raro = weird/rare. 'Qué raro' = how weird / that's strange.
'Normal' = same for masc/fem. 'Con normalidad' = normally. 'Es lo normal' = it's the norm.
'Especial' = same for masc/fem. 'Nada especial' = nothing special. 'Día especial' = special day.
importante = same for masc/fem. 'Lo más importante' = the most important thing.
'Es necesario + infinitive' = it's necessary to. 'Si fuera necesario' = if necessary.
'¿Es posible?' = Is it possible? 'Hacer todo lo posible' = to do everything possible.

levels.front: Is it possible to change it?

levels.grammarNote: difícil = same for masc/fem. 'Más fácil de lo que parece' = easier than it looks.

levels.front: Spanish is not as difficult as it seems.
levels.back: El español no es tan difícil como parece.

levels.grammarNote: 'Suficiente' = sufficient/enough. 'Bastante' = quite/enough. '¡Ya es suficiente!' = That's enough!

levels.back: Con eso es suficiente por ahora.
levels.literal: With that [it] is sufficient for now.

levels.grammarNote: 'Parecido' = similar. 'Distinto' = different. 'Es igual' = it's the same. 'No es lo mismo' = it's not the same.
'Es verdad' = it's true. '¿De verdad?' = Really? 'En serio' = seriously. 'Mentira' = lie.
feliz = deeply happy. contento = content/pleased (weaker). Both use estar. '¡Qué feliz me hace!' = How happy it makes me!
triste = same for masc/fem. Uses estar. 'Estar triste por algo' = to be sad about something.
enojado (LatAm). enfadado (Spain). Uses estar. 'Enojarse' = to get angry.
'Asustarse' = to get scared. '¡No te asustes!' = Don't be scared!

levels.back: Le tengo miedo a las arañas.

levels.grammarNote: 'Sorprenderse' = to be surprised. '¡Sorpresa!' = Surprise! 'Para mi sorpresa' = to my surprise.
emocionado can mean excited OR moved (emotionally). '¡Estoy muy emocionado!' = I'm so excited / I'm so moved.

levels.front: I'm so excited about the trip.
levels.back: Estoy muy emocionado con el viaje.

levels.grammarNote: 'Estar orgulloso de' = to be proud of. 'Me siento orgulloso' = I feel proud.
'Darle vergüenza' = to be embarrassed. 'Me da vergüenza' = I'm embarrassed. 'Vergüenza ajena' = second-hand embarrassment.
'Tener celos' = to be jealous. 'Dar celos' = to make someone jealous. 'Ponerse celoso' = to get jealous.
'Estar enamorado de' = to be in love with. 'Enamorarse de' = to fall in love with.

levels.front: She's head over heels in love.

levels.grammarNote: 'Sentirse solo' = to feel lonely. 'Estar solo' = to be alone. 'La soledad' = loneliness/solitude.
'Estar confundido' = to be confused. 'Confundirse' = to make a mistake/get confused.
'Estar preocupado por' = to be worried about. 'No te preocupes' = don't worry. 'Preocuparse' = to worry.
'Estar relajado' = to be relaxed. 'Relajarse' = to relax. '¡Relájate!' = Relax!
'Estar estresado' = to be stressed. 'El estrés' = stress. 'Quitarse el estrés' = to de-stress.
'Tener esperanza' = to have hope. 'Perder la esperanza' = to lose hope. 'Con esperanza' = hopefully.
desilusionado = disappointed (lost an illusion). decepcionado = disappointed (let down). Both very common.
'Estar agradecido por' = to be grateful for. 'Te lo agradezco mucho' = I really appreciate it.

levels.front: I'm very grateful for your help.
levels.back: Estoy muy agradecido por tu ayuda.

levels.grammarNote: 'Sentirse culpable' = to feel guilty. 'Ser culpable de' = to be guilty of. '¿Quién es el culpable?' = Who's responsible?
'Tener miedo (de/a)' = to be afraid of. 'Dar miedo' = to scare someone. 'Muerto de miedo' = scared to death.

levels.back: Le tengo miedo a las alturas.

levels.grammarNote: '¡Qué alegría!' = How wonderful! (expressing delight). 'Saltar de alegría' = to jump for joy.

levels.front: What a joy to see you!

levels.grammarNote: rabia = rage/fury. 'Me da rabia' = it makes me angry. ira = wrath (more literary). 'Dar rabia' = to infuriate.
'Dar vergüenza' = to cause embarrassment. 'Tener vergüenza' = to feel shame. 'Sin vergüenza' = shameless.
'Tener envidia' = to be envious. 'Dar envidia' = to make someone jealous. '¡Qué envidia!' = I'm so jealous! (said of something desirable).

levels.front: I'm so jealous of your vacation!

levels.grammarNote: 'No perder la esperanza' = not to lose hope. 'Esperanza de vida' = life expectancy. 'Con la esperanza de' = in the hope of.
ánimo = spirit/morale. '¡Ánimo!' = Cheer up! / Come on! 'Estar de buen/mal humor' = to be in a good/bad mood.

levels.back: ¡Ánimo, ya verás que todo sale bien!

levels.grammarNote: 'Tener buena/mala actitud' = to have a good/bad attitude. 'Con actitud positiva' = with a positive attitude.
carácter = character/temperament. personalidad = personality. 'Tener mucho carácter' = to be strong-willed.
paciente = patient (adj) AND patient (noun, in a hospital). 'Tener paciencia' = to have patience.

levels.front: He's very generous with his time.
levels.back: Es muy generoso con su tiempo.

levels.grammarNote: egoísta = same for masc/fem. 'Ser egoísta' = to be selfish.
Both end in -ista = same for masc/fem. 'Ser optimista' = to be an optimist.
'Ser tímido' = to be shy. 'Cohibido' = inhibited/self-conscious.

levels.back: Es muy tímido con los desconocidos.

levels.grammarNote: curioso = curious (wanting to know) AND curious/interesting (describing a thing). '¡Qué curioso!' = How interesting!

levels.front: He has a very creative mind.

levels.grammarNote: perezoso = lazy. vago (informal) = lazy/slacker. 'Tener pereza' = to feel lazy.
trabajador = hardworking (adj) AND worker (noun). 'Ser muy trabajador' = to be very hardworking.
responsable = same for masc/fem. 'Hacerse responsable' = to take responsibility.
'De confianza' = trustworthy/reliable. 'Fiable' = reliable (more formal). 'En quien confiar' = someone to trust.
listo = clever/smart (Spain). Also: listo = ready ('¿estás listo?' = are you ready?). Context matters!
terco = stubborn. cabezona/cabezonería = stubbornness (informal). 'No seas terco' = don't be stubborn.
flexible = same for masc/fem. 'Ser flexible' = to be flexible.

levels.front: You need to be more flexible.

levels.grammarNote: valiente = brave. 'El valor' = courage. 'Hay que tener valor' = you have to have courage.
sincero = sincere/honest. 'A decir verdad' = to tell the truth. 'Ser sincero' = to be honest.

levels.front: I want to be honest with you.

levels.grammarNote: alegre = cheerful/happy (personality). 'Estar alegre' = to be in good spirits. 'Una persona alegre' = a cheerful person.
maduro = mature (person) AND ripe (fruit). 'Madurar' = to mature or to ripen.

levels.front: He's very mature for his age.
levels.back: Es muy maduro para su edad.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cuidar el medio ambiente' = to protect the environment. 'Medioambiental' = environmental.

levels.back: Hay que cuidar el medio ambiente.

levels.grammarNote: tierra = earth (planet), ground, land, soil. 'La Tierra' (capital) = planet Earth. 'Tierra firme' = solid ground.
cielo = sky AND heaven. '¡Cielos!' = Heavens! (exclamation). 'Rascacielos' = skyscraper (sky-scraper).

levels.front: The sky is very blue today.
levels.back: Hoy el cielo está muy azul.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hacer sol' = to be sunny. 'Salida/puesta de sol' = sunrise/sunset. '¡Hace un sol!' = It's so sunny!
'Luna llena' = full moon. 'Media luna' = half moon / crescent. 'Estar en la luna' = to be daydreaming.
'Estrella de mar' = starfish. 'Estrella fugaz' = shooting star. 'Estar bajo las estrellas' = to be under the stars.

levels.front: I can see the stars clearly.

levels.grammarNote: mar can be masculine or feminine in Spanish (el mar / la mar). 'La mar de' = a lot of (informal). 'Hacerse a la mar' = to set sail.
'Río abajo' = downstream. 'Río arriba' = upstream. 'Cuando el río suena, agua lleva' = where there's smoke there's fire.

levels.front: The river is very deep here.
levels.back: El río es muy profundo aquí.

levels.front: We went swimming in the lake.
levels.back: Fuimos a nadar en el lago.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ir a la montaña' = to go to the mountains. 'De montaña' = mountain (adj). 'Sierra' = mountain range.

levels.front: The mountains are covered in snow.
levels.back: Las montañas están cubiertas de nieve.

levels.grammarNote: bosque = forest. selva = jungle/tropical forest. 'Bosque tropical' = tropical forest. 'Perderse en el bosque' = to get lost in the forest.

levels.front: The forest is very dense here.
levels.back: El bosque es muy denso aquí.

levels.grammarNote: selva = jungle AND rainforest. 'La selva amazónica' = the Amazon rainforest. 'La ley de la selva' = the law of the jungle.
'El desierto del Sahara'. 'Desierto de ideas' = devoid of ideas (fig). 'Predicar en el desierto' = to talk to a brick wall.

levels.front: The desert is very hot during the day.
levels.back: El desierto es muy caluroso de día.

levels.grammarNote: campo = countryside, field, and camp. 'Ir al campo' = to go to the countryside. 'Campo de fútbol' = football pitch.

levels.front: I love the peace of the countryside.
levels.back: Me encanta la tranquilidad del campo.

levels.grammarNote: 'Las Islas Canarias' = the Canary Islands. 'Isla desierta' = desert island.

levels.front: I want to visit a tropical island.

levels.grammarNote: 'Estar en las nubes' = to have one's head in the clouds. 'En la nube' = in the cloud (technology).

levels.front: There are a lot of clouds today.

levels.grammarNote: 'Llueve' = it's raining. 'Llover a cántaros' = to rain cats and dogs. 'Bajo la lluvia' = in the rain.
'Nevar' = to snow. 'Copos de nieve' = snowflakes. 'Muñeco de nieve' = snowman.
'Hace viento' = it's windy. 'Viento en popa' = full speed ahead (lit: wind at stern). 'Vientos de cambio' = winds of change.
'Tormenta eléctrica' = thunderstorm. 'Calmarse la tormenta' = for the storm to pass. 'Después de la tormenta, calma' = after the storm comes calm.

levels.front: There was an earthquake last night.

levels.grammarNote: clima = climate AND general atmosphere/feel. 'Cambio climático' = climate change. 'Clima templado' = temperate climate.

levels.front: The climate here is very mild.
levels.back: El clima aquí es muy templado.

levels.grammarNote: 'Contaminación del aire' = air pollution. 'Contaminar' = to pollute/contaminate.

levels.front: Air pollution is a big problem.
levels.back: La contaminación del aire es un gran problema.

levels.grammarNote: 'Reciclar' = to recycle. 'Contenedor de reciclaje' = recycling bin. 'Reducir, reutilizar, reciclar' = reduce, reuse, recycle.
'Energía renovable' = renewable energy. 'Energía solar' = solar energy. 'Lleno de energía' = full of energy.

levels.back: La energía solar es el futuro.

levels.grammarNote: planta = plant (botanical) AND floor/storey. 'Planta baja' = ground floor. 'Planta de interior' = houseplant.

levels.front: I love having plants at home.
levels.back: Me encantan las plantas en casa.

levels.grammarNote: 'Árbol genealógico' = family tree. 'Árbol de Navidad' = Christmas tree. 'Ver el árbol pero no el bosque' = can't see the wood for the trees.
'Estar en flor' = to be in bloom. 'Florecer' = to bloom/flourish. 'Flor y nata' = the cream of the crop.
'Hace un frío de perros' = it's freezing (lit: dog cold). 'Vida de perros' = miserable life. 'El mejor amigo del hombre' = man's best friend.
'Buscarle tres pies al gato' = to make things complicated. 'Hay gato encerrado' = there's something fishy going on.

levels.back: Mi gato duerme todo el día.

levels.grammarNote: pájaro = bird (general, informal). ave = bird (formal, especially for large birds). 'Ave rapaz' = bird of prey.

levels.back: Ese pájaro tiene colores muy bonitos.

levels.grammarNote: pez = live fish. pescado = fish as food. 'Sentirse como pez en el agua' = to feel right at home.

levels.front: The fish in this river are huge.
levels.back: Los peces de este río son enormes.

levels.grammarNote: 'A caballo' = on horseback. 'Montar a caballo' = to ride a horse. 'A caballo regalado no le mires el diente' = don't look a gift horse in the mouth.

levels.front: Do you know how to ride a horse?

levels.grammarNote: 'Las vacas sagradas' = sacred cows (fig). 'Vaca lechera' = dairy cow. 'Estar como una vaca' = to be very fat (informal, rude).

levels.front: The cows are in the field.
levels.back: Las vacas están en el campo.

levels.grammarNote: bicho = bug/creature (informal). 'Bichos raros' = strange creatures/weird people. '¡Qué bicho!' = What a creature!

levels.back: Le tengo miedo a los insectos.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hacer fuego' = to make fire. '¿Tienes fuego?' = Do you have a light? (for a cigarette). 'Poner la mano en el fuego' = to vouch for someone.
'Dejar sin piedra por mover' = to leave no stone unturned. 'Piedra preciosa' = precious stone. 'De piedra' = speechless (quedarse de piedra).

levels.front: The path is full of stones.
levels.back: El camino está lleno de piedras.

levels.grammarNote: profesión = profession (requiring training). oficio = trade/craft. '¿A qué te dedicas?' = What do you do for work?

levels.front: What do you do for a living?

levels.grammarNote: carrera = career AND race AND university degree. 'Hacer una carrera' = to pursue a career/degree. 'Carrera universitaria' = university degree.

levels.front: She has a great career ahead.
levels.back: Tiene una gran carrera por delante.

levels.grammarNote: 'Currículum vítae' (CV). 'Enviar el currículum' = to send one's resume.

levels.front: I need to update my resume.

levels.grammarNote: entrevista = interview (job) AND interview (journalism). 'Entrevistarse con' = to have an interview with.
'Firmar un contrato' = to sign a contract. 'Contrato temporal' = temporary contract. 'Contrato indefinido' = permanent contract.
sueldo is more common in everyday speech. 'Subida de sueldo' = pay raise. 'A sueldo' = salaried.
'Turno de mañana/tarde/noche' = morning/afternoon/night shift. '¿De qué turno eres?' = Which shift are you on?
'Trabajar en la oficina' vs 'trabajar desde casa' (work from home). 'Oficina en casa' = home office.

levels.front: I work from home on Fridays.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tener una reunión' = to have a meeting. 'Reunión de trabajo' = work meeting. '¿A qué hora es la reunión?' = When is the meeting?

levels.back: La reunión empieza a las diez.

levels.grammarNote: 'Proyecto de trabajo' = work project. 'Proyecto de vida' = life plan. 'Aprobar un proyecto' = to approve a project.

levels.front: We're working on a new project.
levels.back: Estamos trabajando en un proyecto nuevo.

levels.grammarNote: informe = report/report document. 'Redactar un informe' = to write a report. 'Informe anual' = annual report.
meta = goal (figurative) AND finish line (literal). objetivo = objective. 'Cumplir una meta' = to achieve a goal.

levels.front: My goal is to be fluent.
levels.back: Mi meta es hablar con fluidez.
levels.literal: My goal is to-speak with fluency.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tener éxito' = to be successful. 'Un éxito' = a hit/success. 'Con éxito' = successfully. 'El éxito no llega solo' = success doesn't come on its own.
'Un fracaso' = a failure/flop. 'Fracasar' = to fail. Different from 'fallar' (to fail/break down) or 'suspender' (to fail an exam).
aula is feminine but uses 'el': 'el aula'. clase = class (the lesson or the room).
lección = lesson (content). clase = class (session or group). 'Dar clase' = to teach a class.
tarea = homework AND task/chore. 'Hacer los deberes / la tarea' = to do homework.
'Hacer un examen' = to take an exam. 'Aprobar/suspender un examen' = to pass/fail an exam. 'Examen oral' = oral exam.
nota = grade (school) AND note (music/document). 'Sacar buenas notas' = to get good grades.
aprobar = to pass. suspender = to fail (Spain). reprobar (LatAm) = to fail. 'Aprobé el examen' = I passed the exam.
título = degree (academic) AND title (of a book) AND deed (property). 'Sacar un título' = to get a degree.

levels.front: I have a degree in economics.

levels.grammarNote: asignatura (Spain) = school subject. materia (LatAm). 'Mi asignatura favorita' = my favorite subject.
historia = history AND story. '¿Cuántas historias!' = What stories! 'Historia de amor' = love story.
'Ciencias naturales' = natural sciences. 'Hombre de ciencia' = scientist. 'A ciencia cierta' = for certain.
Always plural in Spanish: 'las matemáticas'. 'Matemáticas' = math. 'Suma, resta, multiplicación, división' = add, subtract, multiply, divide.

levels.front: I was never good at math.

levels.grammarNote: idioma = language (foreign). lengua = tongue AND language (especially native). 'Lengua materna' = mother tongue.

levels.front: How many languages do you speak?

levels.grammarNote: arte is masculine in singular (el arte) but feminine in plural (las artes). 'Arte moderno' = modern art.
'Poner música' = to put on music. 'Música en directo' = live music. '¿Qué tipo de música te gusta?' = What kind of music do you like?

levels.front: What kind of music do you like?

levels.grammarNote: 'Hacer deporte' = to do sport. 'Deportista' = athlete/sporty person. '¿Practicas algún deporte?' = Do you play any sport?
'Tener conocimientos de' = to have knowledge of. 'A mi entender' = to my understanding. 'El árbol del conocimiento' = the tree of knowledge.

levels.front: He has great knowledge of history.
levels.back: Tiene un gran conocimiento de la historia.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tener habilidad para' = to have a skill for. 'Habilidades blandas' = soft skills. 'Habilidades técnicas' = technical skills.
'Tener experiencia en' = to have experience in. 'Con experiencia' = experienced. 'Aprender de la experiencia' = to learn from experience.
'Hacer práctica' = to practice. 'La práctica hace al maestro' = practice makes perfect. 'En la práctica' = in practice.
'Hacer investigación' = to do research. 'Investigar' = to investigate/research. 'Trabajo de investigación' = research paper.
'El descubrimiento de América' = the discovery of America. 'Hacer un descubrimiento' = to make a discovery.

levels.back: España tiene una geografía muy variada.

levels.grammarNote: 'Literatura clásica' = classical literature. 'Obra literaria' = literary work. 'Género literario' = literary genre.

levels.back: La filosofía hace las grandes preguntas.

levels.grammarNote: 'Psicólogo/a' = psychologist. 'Ir al psicólogo' = to see a therapist/psychologist.

levels.back: La psicología nos ayuda a entendernos.

levels.grammarNote: economía = economy AND economics. 'Economía de mercado' = market economy. 'La economía va bien/mal' = the economy is doing well/badly.
derecho = law (the field) AND right (a right you have). ley = a specific law. 'Estudiar derecho' = to study law.
medicina = medicine (field). 'Estudiar medicina' = to study medicine. 'Medicina general' = general medicine.

levels.front: He studied medicine for six years.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ingeniero/a' = engineer. 'Ingeniería civil' = civil engineering. 'Ingeniería informática' = computer science/engineering.
'Arquitecto/a' = architect. 'La arquitectura de la ciudad' = the city's architecture.
política = politics AND policy AND female politician. 'Hacer política' = to do politics. 'Meterse en política' = to get into politics.

levels.front: I don't like talking about politics.
levels.back: No me gusta hablar de política.

levels.grammarNote: 'El gobierno de' = the government of. 'Gobernar' = to govern. 'Cambio de gobierno' = change of government.
'Sociedad civil' = civil society. 'Sociedad de consumo' = consumer society. 'Alta sociedad' = high society.
'Cultura popular' = popular culture. 'Cultura general' = general knowledge. 'Choque cultural' = culture shock.

levels.front: I love learning about other cultures.
levels.back: Me encanta aprender sobre otras culturas.

levels.grammarNote: 'Mantener las tradiciones' = to maintain traditions. 'Tradición oral' = oral tradition. 'De tradición' = traditional.

levels.front: We have to keep our traditions alive.
levels.back: Hay que mantener vivas las tradiciones.

levels.grammarNote: fiesta = party AND holiday/festival. '¡Que empiece la fiesta!' = Let the party begin!

levels.front: The festival attracts thousands of people.
levels.back: El festival atrae a miles de personas.

levels.grammarNote: 'Libertad de religión' = freedom of religion. 'Ser religioso' = to be religious.

levels.front: Religion is important to many people.
levels.back: La religión es importante para muchas personas.

levels.grammarNote: Dios (capital) = the Christian God. dios (lowercase) = a god (any deity). '¡Dios mío!' = My God! / Oh my!
'Creencias religiosas' = religious beliefs. 'Sistema de creencias' = belief system.
'Hacer justicia' = to do justice. 'Pedir justicia' = to demand justice. '¡Que se haga justicia!' = Let justice be done!
'Libertad de expresión' = freedom of speech. 'Libertad de prensa' = freedom of the press. 'Ser libre' = to be free.
'Igualdad de género' = gender equality. 'Igualdad de oportunidades' = equal opportunities.
'Derechos humanos' = human rights. 'Tener derecho a' = to have the right to. 'Es tu derecho' = it's your right.

levels.front: Everyone has the right to education.
levels.back: Todo el mundo tiene derecho a la educación.

levels.grammarNote: 'Asumir la responsabilidad' = to take responsibility. 'Con responsabilidad' = responsibly.
'La comunidad local' = the local community. 'Comunidad autónoma' = autonomous community (Spanish administrative division).

levels.front: It's for the good of the community.
levels.back: Es por el bien de la comunidad.

levels.grammarNote: 'El barrio' = the neighborhood. 'Barrio chino' = Chinatown. 'Ser del barrio' = to be a local.

levels.front: I grew up in this neighborhood.

levels.grammarNote: nación = nation (political/cultural entity). país = country (a state). 'Orgullo nacional' = national pride.
'En el extranjero' = abroad. 'Extranjero/a' = foreigner. 'Lengua extranjera' = foreign language.

levels.back: Lleva años viviendo en el extranjero.

levels.grammarNote: 'Patrimonio de la humanidad' = UNESCO World Heritage. 'Patrimonio cultural' = cultural heritage.

levels.front: This is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
levels.back: Este es un patrimonio de la humanidad.

levels.grammarNote: fútbol = soccer (USA) / football (UK). The #1 sport in Spain and most of LatAm. 'Partido de fútbol' = football match.

levels.front: Do you follow any football team?

levels.grammarNote: 'Tiempo de ocio' = leisure time. 'Actividad de ocio' = leisure activity.

levels.front: What do you do in your free time?

levels.grammarNote: 'Ser voluntario' = to be a volunteer. 'Hacer voluntariado' = to do volunteer work.

levels.back: ONG / organización sin fines de lucro
levels.grammarNote: ONG = organización no gubernamental (NGO). 'Sin fines de lucro' = non-profit.
'En paz' = in peace. 'Dejar en paz' = to leave alone/in peace. 'Proceso de paz' = peace process. 'Descanse en paz' = rest in peace.
