'En guerra' = at war. 'Guerra fría' = Cold War. 'Declarar la guerra' = to declare war.
'Teléfono fijo' = landline. 'Teléfono móvil / celular' = mobile phone. 'Llamar por teléfono' = to call.
móvil (Spain). celular (LatAm). 'Cargar el móvil' = to charge your phone.

levels.front: My phone is out of battery.
levels.back: Se me quedó sin batería el móvil.
levels.literal: Itself to-me was-left without battery the phone.

levels.grammarNote: computadora (LatAm). ordenador (Spain). 'Portátil' = laptop. 'Encender/apagar el ordenador' = to turn on/off the computer.
'Conexión a internet' = internet connection. 'Navegar por internet' = to browse the internet. 'Sin conexión' = no connection.
sitio web = website. página web = web page. '¿Cuál es su página web?' = What's your website?

levels.back: Consulta nuestra página web para más información.

levels.grammarNote: 'Enviar un email' = to send an email. 'Bandeja de entrada' = inbox. 'Adjunto' = attachment.
'Cambiar la contraseña' = to change the password. 'Olvidé mi contraseña' = I forgot my password.
'Descargar una app' = to download an app. 'Aplicación móvil' = mobile app.
'Estar en las redes' = to be on social media. 'Publicar en redes' = to post on social media.

levels.front: Do you use social media much?

levels.grammarNote: 'Mandar un mensaje' = to send a message. 'Mensaje de texto' = text message. 'Dejar un mensaje' = to leave a message.
'Hacer una llamada' = to make a call. 'Llamada perdida' = missed call. 'Llamada a cobro revertido' = collect call.
vídeo (Spain). video (LatAm and increasingly Spain). 'Llamada de video' = video call. 'Subir un video' = to upload a video.
'Sacar / hacer / tomar una foto' = to take a photo (all three are used). 'Álbum de fotos' = photo album.
'Pantalla de inicio' = home screen. 'Pantalla táctil' = touch screen. 'Bloquear la pantalla' = to lock the screen.
'Cargador de batería' = battery charger. 'La batería está baja / sin batería' = the battery is low / dead.

levels.front: My phone is about to die.
levels.back: Se me va a morir el móvil.
levels.literal: Itself to-me is-going to die the phone.

levels.grammarNote: 'Red wifi' = wifi network. 'Contraseña del wifi' = wifi password. 'Conectarse al wifi' = to connect to wifi.
'Encender / apagar la tele' = to turn on/off the TV. 'Tele a la carta' = on-demand TV. 'Ver la tele' = to watch TV.

levels.back: ¿Qué hay en la tele esta noche?
levels.literal: What there-is on the TV this night?

levels.grammarNote: radio is usually feminine in Spain (la radio) but masculine in LatAm (el radio). 'Poner la radio' = to put on the radio.

levels.front: I listen to the radio in the car.
levels.back: Escucho la radio en el coche.

levels.grammarNote: periódico (general). diario = daily newspaper. 'Leer el periódico' = to read the newspaper. 'Kiosko' = newsstand.
'Revista de moda' = fashion magazine. 'Revista científica' = scientific journal.
'Leer un libro' = to read a book. 'Librería' = bookstore. 'Biblioteca' = library. 'Libro de bolsillo' = paperback.

levels.front: I'm reading a very good book.
levels.back: Estoy leyendo un libro muy bueno.

levels.grammarNote: 'Las noticias' = the news (plural). 'Una noticia' = a piece of news. 'Estar al día de las noticias' = to keep up with the news.
'Ver una serie' = to watch a series. 'Engancharse a una serie' = to get hooked on a series.

levels.back: Estoy viendo una serie muy buena.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ver una película' = to watch a movie. 'Ir al cine' = to go to the cinema. 'Película de acción' = action movie.
'Escuchar una canción' = to listen to a song. 'Cantar una canción' = to sing a song. 'Canción favorita' = favorite song.
'Ir a un concierto' = to go to a concert. 'Concierto en directo' = live concert. 'Entradas de concierto' = concert tickets.

levels.front: I went to an amazing concert.

levels.grammarNote: 'Descargar' = to download. 'Subir' = to upload. 'Descarga gratuita' = free download.

levels.front: The download took a long time.

levels.grammarNote: 'Actualizar' = to update. 'Hay una actualización disponible' = there's an update available.

levels.front: I need to update my phone.

levels.grammarNote: error = error (general). fallo = failure/fault/bug. 'Ha habido un error' = there has been an error.
'Datos personales' = personal data. 'Protección de datos' = data protection. 'Plan de datos' = data plan (phone).
'Configuración de privacidad' = privacy settings. 'Política de privacidad' = privacy policy.
seguridad = security AND safety. 'Guardia de seguridad' = security guard. 'Medidas de seguridad' = safety measures.
'IA' = AI (abbreviation). 'Aprendizaje automático' = machine learning.

levels.back: La inteligencia artificial lo está cambiando todo.

levels.grammarNote: 'En el futuro' = in the future. 'El futuro es incierto' = the future is uncertain. 'Futuro prometedor' = promising future.

levels.front: Nobody knows what the future holds.
levels.back: Nadie sabe lo que depara el futuro.
levels.literal: Nobody knows the that [it]-holds the future.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hacer un viaje' = to take a trip. '¡Buen viaje!' = Have a good trip! 'Viaje de negocios' = business trip.
'Turismo rural' = rural tourism. 'Oficina de turismo' = tourist office. 'Temporada turística' = tourist season.

levels.back: El turismo es muy importante aquí.

levels.grammarNote: turista = same for masc/fem. 'Zona turística' = tourist area. 'Trampa para turistas' = tourist trap.

levels.front: The city is full of tourists.
levels.back: La ciudad está llena de turistas.

levels.grammarNote: guía = guide (person or book). 'Guía turístico/a' = tour guide. 'Guía de viaje' = travel guide (book).
hostal = budget hotel/guesthouse. albergue = hostel (dormitory-style). 'Albergue juvenil' = youth hostel.
'Habitación individual' = single room. 'Habitación doble' = double room. 'Habitación con vista al mar' = sea-view room.

levels.front: Do you have a double room available?

levels.grammarNote: reserva (Spain). reservación (LatAm). 'Hacer una reserva' = to make a reservation.
'Hacer el check-in' = to check in. 'Hora de entrada/salida' = check-in/check-out time.

levels.back: El check-in es a las 2 de la tarde.

levels.grammarNote: 'Equipaje de mano' = carry-on. 'Facturar el equipaje' = to check luggage. 'Recogida de equipaje' = baggage claim.
'Pasar por aduanas' = to go through customs. 'Declarar en aduanas' = to declare at customs.
visa (LatAm). visado (Spain). 'Solicitar una visa' = to apply for a visa.

levels.front: Do I need a visa for this country?

levels.grammarNote: 'Vuelo directo' = direct flight. 'Vuelo de conexión' = connecting flight. 'Número de vuelo' = flight number.

levels.back: Mi vuelo es a las 7 de la mañana.

levels.grammarNote: 'Mostrar la tarjeta de embarque' = to show your boarding pass. 'Embarque' = boarding.
retraso (Spain). demora (LatAm). 'El vuelo lleva retraso' = the flight is delayed.

levels.front: The train is delayed by an hour.
levels.back: El tren lleva una hora de retraso.
levels.literal: The train carries an hour of delay.

levels.grammarNote: 'Con escala en' = with a stopover in. 'Vuelo sin escala' = nonstop flight. 'Escala técnica' = technical stopover.

levels.back: ¿Cuál es el tipo de cambio hoy?

levels.grammarNote: 'Sacar dinero del cajero' = to take money from the ATM.
'Hacer una excursión' = to go on an excursion. 'Excursión de un día' = day trip.

levels.front: We're going on an excursion tomorrow.

levels.grammarNote: 'Las ruinas romanas' = Roman ruins. 'Visitar las ruinas' = to visit the ruins.

levels.front: The cathedral is the main monument.
levels.back: La catedral es el monumento principal.

levels.grammarNote: 'El Palacio Real' = the Royal Palace. 'Palacio de congresos' = conference center.
'Castillo medieval' = medieval castle. 'Construir castillos en el aire' = to build castles in the air (daydream).

levels.front: The castle is on top of the hill.
levels.back: El castillo está en lo alto de la colina.

levels.grammarNote: 'Dejar una propina' = to leave a tip. In Spain tips are less expected than in the US; in LatAm it varies.

levels.front: Leave a tip for the waiter.

levels.grammarNote: '¿Cuánto es la entrada?' = How much is entry? 'Entrada gratuita' = free entry.

levels.front: How much is the entrance fee?
The ticket office opens at nine.
levels.back: La taquilla abre a las nueve.

levels.grammarNote: 'Estar perdido' = to be lost. 'Perderse' = to get lost. '¡Estoy perdido!' = I'm lost! 'Objeto perdido' = lost item.

levels.front: I'm lost. Can you help me?

levels.grammarNote: señal = sign AND signal AND deposit. 'Señal de tráfico' = road sign. 'No hay señal' = no signal (phone).
'En caso de emergencia' = in case of emergency. 'Salida de emergencia' = emergency exit. 'Llamar a emergencias' = to call emergency services.

levels.front: Call an ambulance, it's an emergency!
levels.back: ¡Llama a una ambulancia, es una emergencia!
La embajada española está en el centro.

levels.grammarNote: '¡Bienvenido/a!' = Welcome! (agrees with gender). 'Ser bienvenido' = to be welcome.
'Ser un buen anfitrión' = to be a good host. 'País anfitrión' = host country.
huésped = guest (at a hotel or home). 'El huésped' is also used for 'host' in some older usage — context matters.

levels.front: The guests will arrive at eight.
levels.back: Los huéspedes llegarán a las ocho.

levels.grammarNote: 'Buscar alojamiento' = to look for accommodation. 'Alojamiento incluido' = accommodation included.
Irregular yo: agradezco. 'Te lo agradezco' = I appreciate it / thank you for that. More heartfelt than 'gracias'.
'Disculpa' = excuse me / sorry (informal). 'Perdona' = excuse me (Spain). 'Perdón' = sorry/excuse me. 'Te pido perdón' = I ask your forgiveness.

levels.front: Excuse me, can you help me?

levels.grammarNote: rechazar = to refuse/reject. 'Negarse a' = to refuse to (do something). 'Rechazar una oferta' = to turn down an offer.
Irregular yo: ofrezco. 'Ofrecer ayuda' = to offer help. '¿Te ofrezco algo?' = Can I get you something?

levels.front: Can I offer you a drink?

levels.grammarNote: Stem-changes e→ie: sugiero. 'Te sugiero que' = I suggest that you. '¿Qué me sugieres?' = What do you suggest?
elegir stem-changes e→i: elijo, eliges, elige. 'Elegir entre' = to choose between. 'Tú eliges' = you choose.

levels.back: Tú elige, a mí me da igual.

levels.grammarNote: 'Decidirse a + infinitive' = to make up one's mind to. 'Tomar una decisión' = to make a decision.
Stem-changes o→ue: resuelvo. 'Resolver un problema' = to solve a problem. '¿Cómo lo resolviste?' = How did you solve it?

levels.front: We need to solve this problem.

levels.grammarNote: 'Mejorar en algo' = to improve at something. 'Las cosas mejorarán' = things will improve. '¡Que te mejores!' = Get well soon!
'Aumentar el precio' = to increase the price. 'El paro ha aumentado' = unemployment has increased.
Irregular yo: reduzco. 'Reducir el gasto' = to reduce spending. 'Reducir la velocidad' = to slow down.
'Controlarse' = to control oneself. 'Tener bajo control' = to have under control. 'Fuera de control' = out of control.
Preterite: organicé. 'Organizar una fiesta' = to organize a party. 'Estar bien/mal organizado' = to be well/badly organized.
planear = to plan (Spain). planificar = to plan more formally. '¿Tienes planeado algo?' = Have you got anything planned?

levels.front: What have you planned for the weekend?
levels.back: ¿Qué tienes planeado para el fin de semana?

levels.grammarNote: 'Confirmar una reserva' = to confirm a reservation. '¿Puedes confirmar?' = Can you confirm?
'Cancelar una cita' = to cancel an appointment. 'Vuelo cancelado' = cancelled flight.

levels.front: I have to cancel the meeting.

levels.grammarNote: aplazar = to postpone/defer. 'Aplazar una decisión' = to defer a decision.
'Permitir + infinitive' = to allow to. 'No está permitido' = it's not allowed. 'Con su permiso' = with your permission.

levels.front: You're not allowed to smoke here.

levels.grammarNote: 'Prohibido' = forbidden/prohibited. 'Se prohíbe' = it is forbidden. 'Queda prohibido' = it is strictly prohibited.
Stem-changes e→ie: recomiendo. 'Te lo recomiendo' = I recommend it to you. '¿Qué recomiendas?' = What do you recommend?
Stem-changes e→ie: advierto. 'Advertir de un peligro' = to warn of a danger. 'Te advierto' = I'm warning you.
'Felicitar a alguien' = to congratulate someone. '¡Te felicito!' = Congratulations! 'Felicitaciones' = congratulations (LatAm).
'Invitar a alguien' = to invite someone. 'Te invito' = I'll treat you / it's my treat. 'Invitar a cenar' = to take someone to dinner.
'Sorprender a alguien' = to surprise someone. 'Me sorprende' = it surprises me. '¡Sorpresa!' = Surprise!

levels.front: She surprised him with a gift.

levels.grammarNote: Irregular yo: convenzo. 'Convencer a alguien de algo' = to convince someone of something. 'Déjate convencer' = let yourself be convinced.
'Dudar de' = to doubt. 'Sin dudar' = without hesitation. 'No lo dudo' = I have no doubt about it.
'Evitar hacer algo' = to avoid doing something. 'No hay forma de evitarlo' = there's no way to avoid it.
'Imagínate' = imagine (command). 'Me imagino que' = I imagine that. 'No te lo imaginas' = you can't imagine.
Stem-changes o→ue: sueño, sueñas. 'Soñar con' = to dream about. 'Sueña en grande' = dream big. '¡Ni en sueños!' = Not in a million years!

levels.front: I dreamed about you last night.

levels.grammarNote: 'Lograr + infinitive' = to manage to do / to achieve doing. '¡Lo lograste!' = You did it!

levels.front: I finally managed to do it.

levels.grammarNote: 'Luchar por' = to fight for. 'Luchar contra' = to fight against. 'Lucha libre' = wrestling (Mexico).
'Sufrir de' = to suffer from. 'Sufrir una pérdida' = to suffer a loss.
'Celebrar un aniversario' = to celebrate an anniversary. '¡Hay que celebrarlo!' = We must celebrate this!
'Depende' = it depends (very common answer). 'Depende de ti' = it's up to you.
notar = to notice/feel. fijarse en = to notice/pay attention. '¿Te fijaste?' = Did you notice?
'Desear algo' = to wish for something. 'Te deseo lo mejor' = I wish you all the best. 'Como desees' = as you wish.

levels.front: I wish you all the best.

levels.grammarNote: 'Darse cuenta de' = to realize. 'Me di cuenta de que' = I realized that. Very common expression!

levels.back: Acabo de darme cuenta de que lo olvidé.
levels.literal: [I]-finish of to-give-myself account of that it [I]-forgot.

levels.grammarNote: Irregular yo: parezco. 'Parece que' = it seems that. '¿Qué te parece?' = What do you think? 'Parecerse a' = to look like (resemble).
Irregular yo: pertenezco. '¿A quién pertenece esto?' = Who does this belong to?
'Perder el tren' = to miss the train. 'No te lo pierdas' = don't miss it (event).
'Encontrarse con alguien' = to run into someone / to meet up with. 'Me lo encontré por la calle' = I ran into him on the street.

levels.front: I ran into her at the market.
levels.back: Me la encontré en el mercado.

levels.grammarNote: 'Acostumbrarse a' = to get used to. 'Ya me he acostumbrado' = I'm used to it now. 'Estar acostumbrado a' = to be used to.

levels.front: It takes time to get used to.

levels.grammarNote: 'Aprovechar la oportunidad' = to take advantage of the opportunity. '¡Aprovecha!' = Make the most of it!

levels.front: Make the most of your time here.

levels.grammarNote: manejar = to manage AND to drive (LatAm). gestionar = to manage/handle (admin). '¿Cómo lo manejas?' = How do you handle it?
-ir verb. 'Compartir con' = to share with. 'Compartir piso' = to share a flat. '¿Compartimos?' = Shall we share?
'¿Qué significa?' = What does it mean? '¿Qué quieres decir?' = What do you mean? Both very common.
Irregular yo: traduzco. 'Traducir del español al inglés' = to translate from Spanish to English. 'Traducción' = translation.

levels.front: Can you translate this for me?

levels.grammarNote: durar = to last (duration). tardar = to take time. '¿Cuánto tarda?' = How long does it take? '¿Cuánto dura?' = How long does it last?
Stem-changes o→ue: cuesta, cuestan. '¿Cuánto cuesta?' = How much does it cost? 'Costar trabajo' = to be hard/difficult.
Stem-changes e→i: mido. '¿Cuánto mides?' = How tall are you? 'Medir las consecuencias' = to weigh the consequences.
'¿Cuánto pesas?' = How much do you weigh? 'Pesar el equipaje' = to weigh the luggage. 'Pesar mucho' = to weigh a lot AND to matter a lot.
Stem-changes o→ue: suena. 'Suena bien' = it sounds good. 'Me suena' = it rings a bell / sounds familiar.
Stem-changes o→hue: huelo, hueles, huele. 'Oler bien/mal' = to smell good/bad. '¡Qué bien huele!' = How good that smells!
tocar = to touch (physical contact). sentir = to feel (emotion, sensation). 'No lo toques' = don't touch it.
Stem-changes o→ue: pruebo. probar = to try/taste food AND to try on clothes AND to test. Very versatile!
mostrar stem-changes o→ue: muestro. enseñar = to teach AND to show. Both mean to show.
Stem-changes o→ue: duele, duelen. Used like 'gustar': 'me duele la cabeza' = my head hurts. '¿Te duele?' = Does it hurt?
The same as missing a person: 'echo de menos España' = I miss Spain. 'Extraño la comida' = I miss the food (LatAm).

levels.back: Echo de menos la comida española.

levels.grammarNote: Irregular yo: merezco. 'Mereces lo mejor' = you deserve the best. '¡Lo mereces!' = You deserve it!
'Molestar' = to bother. 'Me molesta' = it bothers me. '¿Te molesta si...?' = Do you mind if...?

levels.front: Do you mind if I open the window?

levels.grammarNote: 'Fallar' = to fail (a machine, a person). 'El motor ha fallado' = the engine has failed. 'No me falles' = don't let me down.
arreglar = to fix AND to tidy up AND to sort out. reparar = to repair (more technical). 'Arreglarlo' = to fix it.
Irregular: construyo, construyes, construye. 'Construir una casa' = to build a house. 'Construirse' = to be built.
Irregular: destruyo. 'Destruir' = to destroy. 'Autodestruirse' = to self-destruct.
ahorrar = to save money. salvar = to rescue/save a life. 'Ahorrar tiempo' = to save time.

levels.front: I'm saving money for a trip.
levels.back: Estoy ahorrando dinero para un viaje.

levels.grammarNote: 'Gastar dinero en' = to spend money on. '¡Cuánto has gastado!' = How much you've spent!
ganar = to earn AND to win. 'Ganar un sueldo' = to earn a salary. 'Ganar la vida' = to earn a living.

levels.front: She earns a very good salary.

levels.grammarNote: contratar = to hire (a person or service). alquilar = to rent (property, a car).

levels.front: We need to hire a plumber.

levels.grammarNote: 'Despedir a alguien' = to fire/let go. 'Despedirse de' = to say goodbye to. 'Despedida' = farewell.
'Pedir prestado' = to borrow (lit: to ask for on loan). 'Prestar' = to lend. '¿Me lo prestas?' = Can I borrow it?
'Prestar dinero' = to lend money. 'Prestar atención' = to pay attention. 'Prestar ayuda' = to provide help.

levels.front: Can you lend me some money?

levels.grammarNote: 'Conocer a alguien' = to meet someone for the first time. 'Mucho gusto en conocerte' = Nice to meet you.
'Saludar a alguien' = to greet someone. '¡Saluda de mi parte!' = Say hi from me!
'Despedirse de alguien' = to say goodbye to someone. 'Sin despedirse' = without saying goodbye.
'Quejarse de algo/alguien' = to complain about something/someone. 'Sin quejarse' = without complaining.
'Estoy de acuerdo' = I agree. 'No estoy de acuerdo' = I disagree. '¿Estamos de acuerdo?' = Do we agree?
'No estoy de acuerdo' is most common. 'Disentir' = to dissent (formal).

levels.back: No estoy de acuerdo con eso.

levels.grammarNote: 'Perdona que te interrumpa' = sorry to interrupt. 'No me interrumpas' = don't interrupt me.
'Entenderse con alguien' = to get along / communicate well with someone. 'Nos entendemos' = we understand each other.
'Tener razón' = to be right. 'No tener razón' = to be wrong. 'Llevas razón' = you're right (Spain). Different from 'estar en lo correcto'.

levels.front: To feel like / look forward to
levels.grammarNote: 'Tener ganas de + infinitive' = to feel like doing. 'Tengo muchas ganas de verte' = I can't wait to see you.
'Tener prisa' = to be in a hurry. 'Date prisa' = hurry up. 'A toda prisa' = in a great hurry.
'Tener suerte' = to be lucky. '¡Qué suerte!' = How lucky! 'Suerte' = luck AND good luck (farewell).

levels.front: To bear in mind / take into account
levels.grammarNote: 'Tener en cuenta' = to take into account / keep in mind. 'Tenlo en cuenta' = keep it in mind.

levels.front: Bear in mind that it's expensive.
levels.back: Ten en cuenta que es caro.
levels.literal: Have in account that [it] is expensive.

levels.grammarNote: 'Darse cuenta de' = to realize. 'Me di cuenta de que' = I realized that. One of the most important phrases to know!

levels.back: Me di cuenta de que estaba equivocado.

levels.grammarNote: 'Dejar de + infinitive' = to stop doing something. 'Deja de quejarte' = stop complaining. 'Dejé de fumar' = I quit smoking.
'Acabar de + infinitive' = to have just done. 'Acabo de llegar' = I just arrived. 'Acabamos de comer' = we just ate.
'Ponerse a + infinitive' = to start doing (often suddenly). 'Se puso a llorar' = he/she started crying. 'Me puse a estudiar' = I started studying.
'Pasarlo bien' = to have a good time. '¿Lo pasaste bien?' = Did you have a good time? 'Pasárselo en grande' = to have a great time.

levels.front: Did you have a good time?

levels.grammarNote: hacerse = to become (by effort): médico, rico. volverse = to become (personality change): nervioso, loco. llegar a ser = to become (achievement over time).
'Date prisa' = hurry up (Spain). 'Apúrate' = hurry up (LatAm). 'Sin prisa' = without hurry / take your time.
'Me importa' = it matters to me. 'No me importa' = I don't mind / I don't care. '¿Qué más da?' = What does it matter?

levels.front: To not care / be all the same
levels.grammarNote: 'Me da igual' = I don't mind / it's all the same to me. 'Da lo mismo' = it doesn't matter / same thing.
'Vale la pena' = it's worth it. '¿Vale la pena?' = Is it worth it? 'No vale la pena' = it's not worth it.

levels.front: How are things? / How's it going?
levels.grammarNote: '¿Qué tal?' = How are you? / How's it going? One of the most common greetings! '¿Qué tal todo?' = How's everything?
'¡Qué bueno!' = How great! (LatAm). '¡Qué bien!' = Great! How nice! Both express positive surprise or pleasure.
'¡Qué pena!' = What a shame! Also used sincerely or sarcastically. '¡Qué lástima!' = What a pity!
'¡Qué va!' = No way! / Of course not! Expresses strong disagreement or disbelief. Very colloquial, very common.
'¡Qué horror!' = How awful! How terrible! '¡Qué espanto!' = How dreadful!
'A lo mejor' always takes indicative (not subjunctive): 'a lo mejor viene' (maybe he'll come). Compare: quizás/tal vez (can take subjunctive).
'En realidad' = in reality / actually. 'La realidad es que' = the reality is that.

levels.back: En realidad, no estoy de acuerdo.

levels.grammarNote: 'En fin' = anyway / well / in short (often a mild resignation). 'En fin, ¿qué le vamos a hacer?' = Oh well, what can you do?

levels.front: Oh well, what can you do?
levels.back: En fin, ¿qué le vamos a hacer?
levels.literal: In end, what to-it [we]-go to make?

levels.grammarNote: 'Al final' = in the end / eventually. 'Al final resulta que' = it turns out that. 'Al fin y al cabo' = after all.

levels.front: In the end it all worked out.

levels.back: de todas formas / de todas maneras
levels.grammarNote: Both mean anyway / regardless. Very interchangeable. 'De todos modos' is another synonym.
'A propósito' = by the way / on purpose. 'Por cierto' = by the way (topic change). Context makes it clear which meaning.

levels.front: By the way, I saw Juan.
That is to say / I mean
levels.grammarNote: 'Es decir' = that is to say (formal). 'O sea' = I mean / that is (very common in speech). 'O sea que' = so.

levels.front: I mean, it's not that simple.
levels.back: O sea, no es tan sencillo.

levels.grammarNote: 'En cambio' = on the other hand / however. 'Por otro lado' = on the other hand. 'Por una parte... por otra' = on one hand... on the other.

levels.front: I like the city. My partner, on the other hand, prefers the countryside.
levels.back: A mí me gusta la ciudad. Mi pareja, en cambio, prefiere el campo.

levels.grammarNote: 'Sin duda' = without a doubt. 'Sin duda alguna' = without any doubt. 'Indudablemente' = undoubtedly.

levels.front: Without a doubt, it's the best.

levels.grammarNote: 'Por lo tanto' = therefore (formal). 'Así que' = so / therefore (informal). Both very common.

levels.front: So what do we do now?

levels.grammarNote: 'A pesar de + noun/infinitive' = despite. 'A pesar de todo' = despite everything. 'A pesar de que' + clause.

levels.front: Despite the rain, we went out.
levels.back: A pesar de la lluvia, salimos.

levels.grammarNote: Both mean 'as long as' + subjunctive. 'Puedes ir siempre que llegues antes de las diez' = You can go as long as you're back before ten.

levels.front: You can go as long as you call me.
levels.back: Puedes ir siempre que me llames.
a menos que / a no ser que
levels.grammarNote: Both mean 'unless' and take the subjunctive. 'A menos que llueva' = unless it rains. 'A no ser que vengas' = unless you come.
'Para que + subjunctive' = so that / in order that. 'Te lo digo para que lo sepas' = I'm telling you so that you know.
'Dado que' = given that. 'Ya que' = since / given that (more conversational). Both introduce a cause.
'Mientras tanto' = meanwhile / in the meantime. 'Entretanto' = meanwhile (more formal).
'En cuanto llegue' = as soon as he arrives (subjunctive). 'Tan pronto como pueda' = as soon as possible.

levels.front: Call me as soon as you arrive.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cada vez que' = every time that. 'Cada vez que llueve, me alegro' = every time it rains, I'm happy.

levels.front: Every time I see him I get nervous.
levels.back: Cada vez que lo veo me pongo nervioso.

levels.grammarNote: 'Aunque + indicative' = although (fact). 'Aunque + subjunctive' = even if (hypothetical). 'Aunque llueve, voy' (it is raining). 'Aunque llueva, voy' (even if it rains).

levels.back: por mucho que / por más que
levels.grammarNote: Both take subjunctive. 'Por mucho que lo intentes, no podrás' = No matter how much you try, you won't be able to.

levels.front: No matter how much I try, I can't.
levels.back: Por mucho que lo intento, no puedo.

levels.grammarNote: 'Al + infinitive' = upon doing / when doing. 'Al llegar' = upon arriving. 'Al verlo' = upon seeing it. Very elegant construction.

levels.back: en vez de / en lugar de
levels.grammarNote: Both mean instead of. 'En vez de hablar, actúa' = instead of talking, act. 'En lugar de eso' = instead of that.

levels.front: Instead of coffee, I'll have tea.
levels.back: En vez de café, tomaré té.

levels.grammarNote: 'Además de + noun/infinitive' = in addition to / besides. 'Además' alone = also / furthermore / besides.
'Según' = according to. 'Según tú' = according to you. 'Según parece' = apparently (lit: according to how it seems).

levels.front: According to the news, it'll rain.

levels.back: con respecto a / en cuanto a
levels.grammarNote: 'Con respecto a' = with respect to. 'En cuanto a' = as for / regarding. Both introduce the topic being discussed.

levels.front: As for the project, it's going well.
levels.back: En cuanto al proyecto, va bien.
levels.literal: In as-much to-the project, [it] goes well.

levels.grammarNote: 'Gracias a' = thanks to (because of, positive). 'Gracias a ti' = thanks to you. Contrast: 'a causa de' = because of (neutral/negative).

levels.front: Thanks to you, I passed the exam.
levels.back: Gracias a ti, aprobé el examen.

levels.grammarNote: 'Debido a' = due to (formal). 'A causa de' = because of. 'Por' = because of (informal). All introduce a cause.

levels.front: Due to the rain, we stayed home.
levels.back: Debido a la lluvia, nos quedamos en casa.
levels.literal: Due-to the rain, ourselves [we]-stayed in house.

levels.grammarNote: 'Es que...' = the thing is... / it's just that... Used to introduce an explanation or excuse. Very conversational.
'Que yo sepa' + subjunctive = as far as I know. 'Que yo sepa, no ha llegado' = as far as I know, he hasn't arrived.

levels.front: As far as I know, it's free.

levels.grammarNote: 'Poco a poco' = little by little / gradually. Very common expression. 'Poco a poco se aprende' = you learn little by little.

levels.front: Little by little, it gets easier.
levels.back: Poco a poco se va haciendo más fácil.
levels.literal: Little by little itself goes making easier.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tan + adjective/adverb + como' = as... as. 'No es tan difícil como parece' = it's not as hard as it seems.

levels.front: It's not as expensive as I thought.
levels.back: No es tan caro como pensaba.

levels.grammarNote: 'Tanto como' = as much as. 'Tanto hombres como mujeres' = both men and women. 'No estudio tanto como debería' = I don't study as much as I should.

levels.front: I don't sleep as much as I should.

levels.grammarNote: 'Ni... ni...' = neither... nor. 'Ni frío ni calor' = neither cold nor hot. 'No sé ni cómo ni cuándo' = I don't know how or when.
'O... o...' = either... or. 'O viene o no viene' = either he comes or he doesn't. 'O estudias o trabajas' = either you study or you work.

levels.front: Either you come or you don't.

levels.grammarNote: 'No solo... sino también' = not only... but also. 'No solo habla inglés, sino también francés' = not only does he speak English, but also French.

levels.front: Not only is it beautiful, but also cheap.
levels.back: No solo es bonito, sino también barato.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cuanto más estudias, más aprendes' = the more you study, the more you learn. 'Cuanto antes, mejor' = the sooner the better.

levels.front: The more you practice, the better.
As... progresses / as time goes by
levels.grammarNote: 'A medida que + verb' = as... goes on. 'A medida que pasa el tiempo' = as time goes by.

levels.front: As time goes by, it gets easier.
levels.back: A medida que pasa el tiempo, se hace más fácil.

levels.grammarNote: 'Con tal de que + subjunctive' = provided that / as long as. 'Con tal de que llegues a tiempo' = as long as you arrive on time.

levels.front: I'll help, provided you help back.
levels.back: Te ayudo con tal de que me ayudes tú también.

levels.grammarNote: 'En caso de + noun' = in case of. 'En caso de que + subjunctive' = in case. 'En caso de emergencia' = in case of emergency.

levels.front: In case it rains, take an umbrella.
levels.back: En caso de que llueva, lleva un paraguas.
levels.literal: In case of that [it]-rain, carry an umbrella.

levels.grammarNote: 'Antes de que + subjunctive' = before. 'Llámame antes de que llegues' = call me before you arrive. Compare: 'antes de + infinitive' (same subject).
'Después de que + subjunctive' = after (different subjects). Same subject: 'después de + infinitive'. 'Después de que comas' = after you eat.

levels.front: After you eat, we'll go out.

levels.grammarNote: 'Hasta que + subjunctive' (future meaning) or indicative (habit/fact). 'Espera hasta que llegue' = wait until he arrives (subjunctive — not yet happened).
'Desde que + verb' = since. 'No lo he visto desde que se fue' = I haven't seen him since he left. 'Desde cuando' = since when.

levels.front: Since she left, I miss her.
levels.back: Desde que se fue, la echo de menos.
levels.literal: Since that herself [she]-went, her [I]-throw of less.

levels.grammarNote: 'Sin + infinitive' = without doing. 'Sin pensarlo' = without thinking about it. 'Se fue sin decir nada' = he left without saying anything.
'Al + infinitive' = upon/when doing. 'Al abrir la puerta' = upon opening the door. 'Al verla' = when I saw her.

levels.front: When she saw him, she smiled.
I wonder if / Let's see if
levels.grammarNote: 'A ver si' + indicative = let's see if / I wonder if / I hope. 'A ver si puedes venir' = let's see if you can come / I hope you can come.

levels.front: For the record / let it be known
levels.grammarNote: 'Que conste que' = let it be known that / for the record. 'Que conste que yo lo dije' = let it be known that I said so.

levels.front: For the record, I warned you.

levels.grammarNote: 'No me da la gana' = I don't feel like it / I don't want to (emphatic, can be rude). 'Porque no me da la gana' = because I don't want to.

levels.front: I don't feel like going out.
levels.back: No me da la gana de salir.
levels.literal: Not to-me [it]-gives the desire of to-go-out.

levels.grammarNote: '¡Venga!' in Spain = Come on! / Let's go! / Okay then! / Goodbye! It's one of the most versatile words in spoken Spanish.
¡Ándale! (Mexico) = let's go / come on / hurry up. ¡Dale! (Argentina/LatAm) = go for it / okay / come on.

levels.front: No way! / Out of the question!
levels.grammarNote: '¡Ni hablar!' = No way! / Out of the question! Emphatic refusal. '¡Ni se te ocurra!' = Don't even think about it!
'¿Y qué?' = So what? / So? Expresses indifference or mild defiance. '¿Y a mí qué?' = So what do I care?
'Todo a su tiempo' = all in good time / everything in its own time. 'No hay que apresurarse' = no need to rush.
'Cada vez más' = more and more. 'Cada vez menos' = less and less. 'Cada vez mejor' = better and better.
'Más o menos' = more or less / so-so. '¿Cómo estás? — Más o menos' = How are you? — So-so. Very common filler!
Both mean at least. 'Al menos llámame' = at least call me. 'Por lo menos' is slightly more emphatic.
'Como mucho' = at most (informal). 'A lo sumo' = at most (formal). 'Son las cinco como mucho' = it's five o'clock at the most.

levels.front: It'll take two hours at most.

levels.grammarNote: 'Cuanto antes, mejor' = the sooner the better. 'Cuanto antes puedas' = as soon as you can.

levels.back: al fin y al cabo / después de todo
levels.grammarNote: 'Al fin y al cabo' = after all / when all is said and done. 'Después de todo' = after all / despite everything.

levels.back: Al fin y al cabo, es tu amigo.
levels.literal: At-the end and at-the fall, [he] is your friend.

levels.grammarNote: Both mean apparently / it seems. 'Al parecer, no vendrá' = apparently he won't come. 'Por lo visto' = from what I can see / apparently.
'A partir de ahora' = from now on. 'A partir de hoy' = starting today. 'A partir de ese momento' = from that moment on.

levels.front: From now on, things will change.
levels.back: A partir de ahora, las cosas cambiarán.

levels.grammarNote: 'De una vez' = once and for all / right now / finally. 'Hazlo de una vez' = just do it already / do it once and for all.
'Por casualidad' = by chance. '¿Tienes por casualidad...?' = Do you happen to have...? 'Sin querer' = without meaning to / accidentally.

levels.front: Do you happen to have change?

levels.grammarNote: 'A propósito' = on purpose AND by the way. 'Adrede' = on purpose (more emphatic). 'Lo hice sin querer, no a propósito' = I did it by accident, not on purpose.
'Estar a punto de + infinitive' = to be about to. 'Estaba a punto de salir' = I was about to leave.

levels.front: I was about to call you.

levels.back: dar por sentado / dar por hecho
levels.grammarNote: 'Dar por sentado' = to take for granted. 'No des nada por sentado' = don't take anything for granted.
'Tomar el pelo' = to pull someone's leg / to tease. '¿Me estás tomando el pelo?' = Are you pulling my leg?

levels.front: To kill two birds with one stone
levels.back: matar dos pájaros de un tiro
levels.grammarNote: 'Matar dos pájaros de un tiro' = to kill two birds with one stone.

levels.front: Let's kill two birds with one stone.
levels.back: Matemos dos pájaros de un tiro.
levels.literal: Let's-kill two birds of one shot.

levels.front: To cost an arm and a leg
levels.back: costar un ojo de la cara
levels.grammarNote: 'Costar un ojo de la cara' = to cost a fortune (lit: to cost an eye from the face).

levels.back: Este restaurante cuesta un ojo de la cara.
levels.literal: This restaurant costs an eye of the face.

levels.grammarNote: 'Llover a cántaros' = to rain heavily / cats and dogs (lit: to rain jugs). 'Está lloviendo a cántaros' = it's pouring.
'Pasárselo en grande' = to have a great time. 'Pasárselo bomba' (Spain) = to have a blast.
'Quedarse de piedra' = to be speechless/stunned (lit: to become stone). 'Quedarse boquiabierto' = to be dumbfounded.
'Soy todo oídos' = I'm all ears. 'Estoy escuchando' = I'm listening.
'A última hora' = at the last minute / at the last moment. 'Cambio de última hora' = last-minute change.

levels.front: He cancelled at the last minute.

levels.back: de vez en cuando / de higos a brevas
levels.grammarNote: 'De higos a brevas' = once in a blue moon (lit: from figs to figs again). 'Muy de vez en cuando' = very rarely.

levels.front: I only see him once in a blue moon.
levels.back: Lo veo de higos a brevas.

levels.grammarNote: 'Estar en forma' = to be fit. 'Ponerse en forma' = to get fit. 'Estar bien/mal' = to be okay/not okay.

levels.back: así es la vida / qué le vamos a hacer
levels.grammarNote: 'Así es la vida' = such is life / it is what it is. '¿Qué le vamos a hacer?' = What can you do? (resigned acceptance).
'Pase lo que pase' = come what may / whatever happens. 'Cueste lo que cueste' = whatever the cost.

levels.front: I'll be here, come what may.
levels.back: Estaré aquí, pase lo que pase.

levels.front: All that glitters is not gold
levels.back: no todo lo que brilla es oro

levels.front: Not everything is as it seems.
levels.back: No todo lo que brilla es oro.
levels.literal: Not everything the that [it]-shines is gold.

levels.grammarNote: 'De alguna manera' = somehow / in some way. 'De algún modo' = somehow. 'De una manera u otra' = one way or another.

levels.back: De alguna manera, lo haremos funcionar.

levels.grammarNote: 'De repente' = suddenly. 'De pronto' = suddenly / all of a sudden. Both very common in storytelling.
'Sin prisa pero sin pausa' = without haste but without pause. 'Poco a poco' = little by little. All express steady progress.
'Cuanto antes' = as soon as possible / the sooner. 'Hazlo cuanto antes' = do it as soon as you can.

levels.front: Do it as soon as you can.

levels.grammarNote: 'Aprender de memoria' = to learn by heart. 'Saber de memoria' = to know by heart. 'Lo sé de memoria' = I know it by heart.
'En general' = in general. 'Por lo general' = generally / as a rule. 'Por norma general' = as a general rule.

levels.front: Generally, I go to bed early.
levels.back: Por lo general, me acuesto temprano.
por otro lado / por otra parte
levels.grammarNote: 'Por otro lado' = on the other hand. 'Por una parte... por otra' = on one hand... on the other. Common in balanced arguments.

levels.front: On the other hand, it's very comfortable.
levels.back: Por otro lado, es muy cómodo.

levels.grammarNote: 'Como resultado' = as a result. 'Por consiguiente' = consequently (formal). 'Por ende' = therefore (very formal).

levels.front: As a result, we had to leave.

levels.grammarNote: 'Al contrario' = on the contrary. '¿Te molestó? — Al contrario, me encantó' = Did it bother you? — On the contrary, I loved it.

levels.front: On the contrary, I loved it.

levels.grammarNote: 'En resumen' = in summary. 'En pocas palabras' = in a few words / to put it briefly. 'Para resumir' = to summarize.

levels.front: In short, it was a success.

levels.grammarNote: 'Al principio' = at first / in the beginning. 'Desde el principio' = from the start. 'El principio del fin' = the beginning of the end.
'Al final' = in the end (after everything). 'Por fin' = finally (expressing relief). 'Por último' = lastly (in a list).
'Ahora mismo' = right now (Spain). 'En este momento' = at this moment. 'Ya mismo' = right now (LatAm).
'Por primera vez' = for the first time. 'La primera vez que' = the first time that.

levels.front: This is my first time here.
levels.back: Es la primera vez que vengo aquí.
levels.literal: [It] is the first time that [I]-come here.

levels.grammarNote: 'Una vez más' = once again / one more time. 'Otra vez' = again. 'De nuevo' = again / anew.
'Al mismo tiempo' = at the same time. 'A la vez' = at the same time / simultaneously. 'De una vez' = all at once.

levels.front: Don't talk at the same time.

levels.grammarNote: 'Poco a poco' = little by little. 'Paso a paso' = step by step. Both used for gradual progress.
'Más o menos' = more or less / approximately / so-so. '¿Cómo estás? — Más o menos' = How are you? — So-so.

levels.back: Está a veinte minutos más o menos.

levels.grammarNote: 'Al menos' = at least. 'Por lo menos' = at least. Both very common. 'Al menos inténtalo' = at least try.
'Tanto + noun' = so much/many. 'No esperaba tanto' = I didn't expect so much. '¡Tantos años sin verte!' = So many years without seeing you!

levels.front: I didn't expect so many people.
To be about / to deal with
levels.grammarNote: '¿De qué trata?' = What is it about? 'La película trata de...' = The movie is about... 'Se trata de' = it's about / it's a matter of.
'Llevarse bien con' = to get along with. 'Entenderse' = to understand each other / get along. '¿Os lleváis bien?' = Do you get along?

levels.front: Do you get along with him?

levels.grammarNote: 'Aguantar' = to put up with / endure. 'Soportar' = to stand / tolerate. 'No lo aguanto' = I can't stand him/it.
'De parte de' = on behalf of / from. '¿De parte de quién?' = Who's calling? (on the phone). 'Saludos de parte de' = greetings from.
Stem-changes o→ue: devuelvo. 'Devolver algo' = to give back / return something. 'Devolver dinero' = to refund. 'Devolver la llamada' = to call back.

levels.front: I need to give this back to you.

levels.grammarNote: 'Resulta que' = it turns out that. 'Resultó ser' = it turned out to be. '¿Cómo resultó?' = How did it turn out?

levels.front: It turns out he was right.

levels.grammarNote: '¡Buen provecho!' = Enjoy your meal! / Bon appétit! Said before/during eating. '¡Que aproveche!' is another version.
'¡Salud!' = Cheers! (toast) AND Bless you! (after sneezing). The same word for both. '¡Salud, dinero y amor!' = Health, wealth and love! (popular toast).
¡Felicidades! = Congratulations! (LatAm, universal). ¡Enhorabuena! = Congratulations! (Spain). Both are correct.
'¡Buena suerte!' = Good luck! '¡Mucha suerte!' = Lots of luck! '¡Suerte!' = Good luck! (shorter).
'¡Que te mejores!' = Get well soon! (informal). '¡Que se mejore!' = Get well soon! (formal). A subjunctive wish structure.
'¡Cuídate!' = Take care! (informal). '¡Cuídese!' = Take care! (formal). Very common farewell.
'¡Disfruta!' = Enjoy! '¡Que lo disfrutes!' = Enjoy it! (with subjunctive wish). '¡Disfrutad!' = Enjoy! (plural).
'Lo siento' = I'm sorry (apology). 'Lo siento mucho' = I'm so sorry. 'Perdón' = Sorry / Excuse me. 'Disculpe' = Excuse me (formal).
'De nada' = you're welcome. 'Con mucho gusto' = with pleasure (LatAm). 'A ti' = thank YOU (casual). 'No hay de qué' = don't mention it.
'No hay de qué' = don't mention it / you're welcome. More formal than 'de nada'.

levels.back: no es nada / no te preocupes
levels.grammarNote: 'No es nada' = it's nothing / don't worry. 'No te preocupes' = don't worry.
'Depende' = it depends. 'Depende de' = it depends on. One of the most honest answers in Spanish! Very common.

levels.front: Does it cost a lot? — It depends.

levels.grammarNote: 'No estoy seguro' = I'm not sure. 'No sé con certeza' = I don't know for certain.
'¡Qué casualidad!' = What a coincidence! '¡Vaya coincidencia!' = What a coincidence! (more emphatic).

levels.front: What a coincidence seeing you here!

levels.back: es una pena / es una lástima
levels.grammarNote: 'Es una pena que' + subjunctive = It's a pity that. 'Qué lástima' = What a shame.

levels.front: It's a shame you can't come.
levels.back: Es una pena que no puedas venir.
levels.literal: [It] is a shame that not [you]-can to-come.

levels.grammarNote: '¡Te lo digo yo!' = I'm telling you! / Trust me! '¡Te lo juro!' = I swear! Both add emphasis and sincerity.

levels.back: ¡Te lo digo yo, vale la pena!

levels.grammarNote: '¡Qué suerte!' = How lucky! '¡Qué suerte tienes!' = Lucky you! 'Tienes mucha suerte' = You're very lucky.
'¡Vaya!' = Well! / Wow! / Oh! Expresses surprise, disappointment, or admiration. '¡Caramba!' = Wow! / Goodness! (milder oath).
'¡Ya era hora!' = It's about time! / About time too! Expresses relief that something finally happened.
'¡Qué raro!' = How strange! 'Es que es muy raro' = it's just very weird. One of the most common exclamations.
'Así es la vida' = that's life / such is life. 'Qué le vamos a hacer' = what can you do (resigned).
'Aquí tienes' = here you are / here you go (handing something). 'Toma' = here (take this). Both when handing something to someone.
'¿Qué te parece?' = What do you think? / How does it seem to you? '¿Qué opinas?' = What's your opinion?

levels.front: What do you think of this?

levels.grammarNote: '¡Eso es!' = That's it! / That's right! '¡Exacto!' = Exactly! Both to affirm what someone said. '¡Justo!' = Exactly! / Just right!
